title: Justin Timberlake

infobox.value: Justin Randall Timberlake   (1981-01-31) January 31, 1981 (age 45)  [Memphis, Tennessee](/wiki/Memphis,_Tennessee "Memphis, Tennessee"), U.S.
* Singer * songwriter * dancer * actor * record producer

* [Discography](/wiki/Justin_Timberlake_discography "Justin Timberlake discography") * [live performances](/wiki/List_of_Justin_Timberlake_live_performances "List of Justin Timberlake live performances") * [production](/wiki/Justin_Timberlake_discography#Production_discography "Justin Timberlake discography") * [videography](/wiki/Justin_Timberlake_videography "Justin Timberlake videography")
[Full list](/wiki/List_of_awards_and_nominations_received_by_Justin_Timberlake "List of awards and nominations received by Justin Timberlake")

(born January 31, 1981)[[1]](#cite_note-grammy-1) is an American singer, songwriter, actor, record producer, and dancer.[[2]](#cite_note-2) Dubbed the "[Prince of Pop](/wiki/Honorific_nicknames_in_popular_music "Honorific nicknames in popular music")", he is one of the [best-selling music artists](/wiki/List_of_best-selling_music_artists "List of best-selling music artists") of all time.[[3]](#cite_note-3) *[Billboard](/wiki/Billboard_(magazine) "Billboard (magazine)")* named him one of the [greatest pop stars of the 21st century](/wiki/Billboard%27s_Greatest_Pop_Stars_of_the_21st_Century "Billboard's Greatest Pop Stars of the 21st Century").[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5) His [awards](/wiki/List_of_awards_and_nominations_received_by_Justin_Timberlake "List of awards and nominations received by Justin Timberlake") include ten [Grammy Awards](/wiki/Grammy_Awards "Grammy Awards"), four [Primetime Emmy Awards](/wiki/Primetime_Emmy_Awards "Primetime Emmy Awards"), three [Brit Awards](/wiki/Brit_Awards "Brit Awards"), nine [*Billboard* Music Awards](/wiki/Billboard_Music_Awards "Billboard Music Awards"), the [Contemporary Icon Award](/wiki/Songwriters_Hall_of_Fame#Contemporary_Icon_Award "Songwriters Hall of Fame") by the [Songwriters Hall of Fame](/wiki/Songwriters_Hall_of_Fame "Songwriters Hall of Fame"), and [MTV](/wiki/MTV "MTV")'s [Michael Jackson Video Vanguard Award](/wiki/Michael_Jackson_Video_Vanguard_Award "Michael Jackson Video Vanguard Award").

**Justin Randall Timberlake** (born January 31, 1981)[[1]](#cite_note-grammy-1) is an American singer, songwriter, actor, record producer, and dancer.[[2]](#cite_note-2) Dubbed the "[Prince of Pop](/wiki/Honorific_nicknames_in_popular_music "Honorific nicknames in popular music")", he is one of the [best-selling music artists](/wiki/List_of_best-selling_music_artists "List of best-selling music artists") of all time.[[3]](#cite_note-3) *[Billboard](/wiki/Billboard_(magazine) "Billboard (magazine)")* named him one of the [greatest pop stars of the 21st century](/wiki/Billboard%27s_Greatest_Pop_Stars_of_the_21st_Century "Billboard's Greatest Pop Stars of the 21st Century").[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5) His [awards](/wiki/List_of_awards_and_nominations_received_by_Justin_Timberlake "List of awards and nominations received by Justin Timberlake") include ten [Grammy Awards](/wiki/Grammy_Awards "Grammy Awards"), four [Primetime Emmy Awards](/wiki/Primetime_Emmy_Awards "Primetime Emmy Awards"), three [Brit Awards](/wiki/Brit_Awards "Brit Awards"), nine [*Billboard* Music Awards](/wiki/Billboard_Music_Awards "Billboard Music Awards"), the [Contemporary Icon Award](/wiki/Songwriters_Hall_of_Fame#Contemporary_Icon_Award "Songwriters Hall of Fame") by the [Songwriters Hall of Fame](/wiki/Songwriters_Hall_of_Fame "Songwriters Hall of Fame"), and [MTV](/wiki/MTV "MTV")'s [Michael Jackson Video Vanguard Award](/wiki/Michael_Jackson_Video_Vanguard_Award "Michael Jackson Video Vanguard Award").

Born and raised in [Memphis, Tennessee](/wiki/Memphis,_Tennessee "Memphis, Tennessee"), Timberlake appeared on musical television shows as a child, competing on *[Star Search](/wiki/Star_Search "Star Search")*; in the early to mid-1990s, he was a cast member on the [Disney Channel](/wiki/Disney_Channel "Disney Channel")'s *[The All New Mickey Mouse Club](/wiki/The_Mickey_Mouse_Club#1989–94_revival:_The_All-New_Mickey_Mouse_Club "The Mickey Mouse Club")*, where he performed cover songs, skits, and choreography. On the show, Timberlake met and worked with future bandmate [JC Chasez](/wiki/JC_Chasez "JC Chasez"), and the two later rose to prominence as the [lead vocalists](/wiki/Lead_vocalist "Lead vocalist") of the [pop](/wiki/Pop_music "Pop music") [boy band](/wiki/Boy_band "Boy band") [NSYNC](/wiki/NSYNC "NSYNC"). The group released four commercially successful albums, becoming one of the [best-selling](/wiki/Best-selling "Best-selling") boy bands of all time with sales of over 70 million units worldwide.

Timberlake's first two solo albums, *[Justified](/wiki/Justified_(album) "Justified (album)")* (2002) and *[FutureSex/LoveSounds](/wiki/FutureSex/LoveSounds "FutureSex/LoveSounds")* (2006), both sold over 10 million copies and received triple platinum certifications from the [Recording Industry Association of America](/wiki/Recording_Industry_Association_of_America "Recording Industry Association of America") (RIAA). The latter also became his first to debut atop the US [*Billboard* 200](/wiki/Billboard_200 "Billboard 200"). His next three albums also peaked on the chart as well: *[The 20/20 Experience](/wiki/The_20/20_Experience "The 20/20 Experience")*, *[The 20/20 Experience – 2 of 2](/wiki/The_20/20_Experience_%E2%80%93_2_of_2 "The 20/20 Experience – 2 of 2")* (both 2013), and *[Man of the Woods](/wiki/Man_of_the_Woods "Man of the Woods")* (2018); the former became the [best-selling album of the year](/wiki/List_of_best-selling_albums_by_year_in_the_United_States "List of best-selling albums by year in the United States"). Timberlake's high-charting singles include "[Cry Me a River](/wiki/Cry_Me_a_River_(Justin_Timberlake_song) "Cry Me a River (Justin Timberlake song)")", "[Rock Your Body](/wiki/Rock_Your_Body "Rock Your Body")", "[SexyBack](/wiki/SexyBack "SexyBack")", "[My Love](/wiki/My_Love_(Justin_Timberlake_song) "My Love (Justin Timberlake song)")", "[LoveStoned/I Think She Knows](/wiki/LoveStoned "LoveStoned")", "[What Goes Around... Comes Around](/wiki/What_Goes_Around..._Comes_Around "What Goes Around... Comes Around")", "[Give It to Me](/wiki/Give_It_to_Me_(Timbaland_song) "Give It to Me (Timbaland song)")", "[4 Minutes](/wiki/4_Minutes "4 Minutes")", "[Pusher Love Girl](/wiki/Pusher_Love_Girl "Pusher Love Girl")", "[Suit & Tie](/wiki/Suit_%26_Tie "Suit & Tie")", "[Mirrors](/wiki/Mirrors_(Justin_Timberlake_song) "Mirrors (Justin Timberlake song)")" and "[Can't Stop the Feeling!](/wiki/Can%27t_Stop_the_Feeling! "Can't Stop the Feeling!")", which earned him an [Academy Award](/wiki/Academy_Award_for_Best_Original_Song "Academy Award for Best Original Song") nomination.

Timberlake enhanced his popularity with [an acting career](/wiki/Justin_Timberlake_videography "Justin Timberlake videography"). He landed starring roles in the films *[The Love Guru](/wiki/The_Love_Guru "The Love Guru")* (2008), *[The Social Network](/wiki/The_Social_Network "The Social Network")* (2010), *[Bad Teacher](/wiki/Bad_Teacher "Bad Teacher")* (2011), *[Friends with Benefits](/wiki/Friends_with_Benefits_(film) "Friends with Benefits (film)")* (2011), *[In Time](/wiki/In_Time "In Time")* (2011), *[Wonder Wheel](/wiki/Wonder_Wheel_(film) "Wonder Wheel (film)")* (2017), and *[Palmer](/wiki/Palmer_(film) "Palmer (film)")* (2021). He has also taken part in [voice acting](/wiki/Voice_acting "Voice acting"), voicing [Artie](/wiki/King_Arthur "King Arthur") in *[Shrek the Third](/wiki/Shrek_the_Third "Shrek the Third")* (2007), [Boo-Boo Bear](/wiki/Boo-Boo_Bear "Boo-Boo Bear") in *[Yogi Bear](/wiki/Yogi_Bear_(film) "Yogi Bear (film)")* (2010), and Branch in the [*Trolls* franchise](/wiki/Trolls_(franchise) "Trolls (franchise)") since 2016. He has frequently collaborated with producers such as [The Neptunes](/wiki/The_Neptunes "The Neptunes") and [Timbaland](/wiki/Timbaland "Timbaland"), who are best known for boosting Timberlake's solo career.

## Early life

Justin Randall Timberlake was born on January 31, 1981, in [Memphis, Tennessee](/wiki/Memphis,_Tennessee "Memphis, Tennessee"),[[1]](#cite_note-grammy-1) the son of Janet Lynn (née Bomar) Harless and Charles Randall Timberlake, a [Baptist](/wiki/Baptist "Baptist") church choir director.[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9) Timberlake has two half-brothers, Jonathan and Stephen, from Charles' second marriage to Lisa Perry. His half-sister Laura Katherine died shortly after birth in 1997 and is mentioned in his acknowledgments in the album *[NSYNC](/wiki/NSYNC_(album) "NSYNC (album)")* as "My Angel in Heaven".

His family circle includes several musicians;[[10]](#cite_note-10) his grandfather introduced him to music from [country music](/wiki/Country_music "Country music") artists like [Johnny Cash](/wiki/Johnny_Cash "Johnny Cash") and [Willie Nelson](/wiki/Willie_Nelson "Willie Nelson").[[11]](#cite_note-rsinterview-11) Performing as a child, Timberlake sang country and [gospel music](/wiki/Gospel_music "Gospel music"): at the age of 11, he appeared on the television show *[Star Search](/wiki/Star_Search "Star Search")*, performing country songs as "Justin Randall".[[12]](#cite_note-12) By that time, he began listening to [rhythm and blues](/wiki/Rhythm_and_blues "Rhythm and blues") musicians from the 1960s and 70s, such as [Al Green](/wiki/Al_Green "Al Green"), [Stevie Wonder](/wiki/Stevie_Wonder "Stevie Wonder"), and [Marvin Gaye](/wiki/Marvin_Gaye "Marvin Gaye"), and he had listening sessions with his father of albums by the [Eagles](/wiki/Eagles_(band) "Eagles (band)") and [Bob Seger](/wiki/Bob_Seger "Bob Seger").[[11]](#cite_note-rsinterview-11)

From 1993 to 1995, he was a Mouseketeer on *[The All New Mickey Mouse Club](/wiki/The_Mickey_Mouse_Club#1989–1994_revival:_The_All-New_Mickey_Mouse_Club "The Mickey Mouse Club")*, where his castmates included future girlfriend and singer [Britney Spears](/wiki/Britney_Spears "Britney Spears"), future tourmate [Christina Aguilera](/wiki/Christina_Aguilera "Christina Aguilera"), future bandmate [JC Chasez](/wiki/JC_Chasez "JC Chasez"), and future actors [Ryan Gosling](/wiki/Ryan_Gosling "Ryan Gosling") and [Keri Russell](/wiki/Keri_Russell "Keri Russell").[[13]](#cite_note-allmusicbio-13) In 1995, Timberlake recruited Chasez to be in an all-male singing group, put together by [Chris Kirkpatrick](/wiki/Chris_Kirkpatrick "Chris Kirkpatrick") and financed by boy band manager [Lou Pearlman](/wiki/Lou_Pearlman "Lou Pearlman"), that eventually became [NSYNC](/wiki/NSYNC "NSYNC").[[14]](#cite_note-14)

## Career

### 1995–2002: NSYNC

Main article: [NSYNC § History](/wiki/NSYNC#History "NSYNC")

The [boy band](/wiki/Boy_band "Boy band") [NSYNC](/wiki/NSYNC "NSYNC") formed in 1995, and they began recording and performing in 1996 in Europe; Timberlake and Chasez served as its two lead singers.[[15]](#cite_note-15) In 1998, the group rose to prominence in the United States with the release of their [self-titled debut album](/wiki/NSYNC_(album) "NSYNC (album)"), which sold 11 million copies[[16]](#cite_note-16) and includes the single "[Tearin' Up My Heart](/wiki/Tearin%27_Up_My_Heart "Tearin' Up My Heart")".[[17]](#cite_note-USRadio-17) The group had a series of legal battles with former manager [Lou Pearlman](/wiki/Lou_Pearlman "Lou Pearlman"),[[18]](#cite_note-Pearlman1-18) and they subsequently signed with [Jive Records](/wiki/Jive_Records "Jive Records").[[19]](#cite_note-19) They released their third album, *[No Strings Attached](/wiki/No_Strings_Attached_(NSYNC_album) "No Strings Attached (NSYNC album)")* (2000), which sold 2.4 million copies in the first week, becoming the fastest-selling album of all time.[[20]](#cite_note-20) They held this record until 2015, when [Adele](/wiki/Adele "Adele") surpassed the record with her third album, *[25](/wiki/25_(Adele_album) "25 (Adele album)")*.[[21]](#cite_note-21) *[No Strings Attached](/wiki/No_Strings_Attached_(NSYNC_album) "No Strings Attached (NSYNC album)")* also includes the number one singles "[It's Gonna Be Me](/wiki/It%27s_Gonna_Be_Me "It's Gonna Be Me")",[[22]](#cite_note-22) "[Bye Bye Bye](/wiki/Bye_Bye_Bye "Bye Bye Bye")",[[23]](#cite_note-usrel-23) and "[This I Promise You](/wiki/This_I_Promise_You "This I Promise You")".[[24]](#cite_note-ET20-24) The same year, Timberlake made his film debut in [The Wonderful World of Disney](/wiki/The_Wonderful_World_of_Disney "The Wonderful World of Disney") film *[Model Behavior](/wiki/Model_Behavior "Model Behavior")*. He played Jason Sharpe, a model who falls in love with a waitress after mistaking her for another model.[[25]](#cite_note-25) He also appeared as a young [Elton John](/wiki/Elton_John "Elton John") in the video for John's song "[This Train Don't Stop There Anymore](/wiki/This_Train_Don%27t_Stop_There_Anymore "This Train Don't Stop There Anymore")" the following year.[[26]](#cite_note-26) NSYNC's fourth album, *[Celebrity](/wiki/Celebrity_(album) "Celebrity (album)")* (2001), was also financially successful, selling 1.8 million copies in the first week[[27]](#cite_note-27) and spawning the top-five single "[Girlfriend](/wiki/Girlfriend_(NSYNC_song) "Girlfriend (NSYNC song)")".[[28]](#cite_note-baker-28) Upon the completion of the [Celebrity Tour](/wiki/Celebrity_Tour "Celebrity Tour"), the group went on an indefinite hiatus in 2002.[[29]](#cite_note-29) NSYNC performed at the [Academy Awards](/wiki/Academy_Awards "Academy Awards") in 2000,[[30]](#cite_note-30) the [2002 Winter Olympics](/wiki/2002_Winter_Olympics "2002 Winter Olympics"),[[31]](#cite_note-31) and the [Super Bowl XXXV halftime show](/wiki/Super_Bowl_XXXV_halftime_show "Super Bowl XXXV halftime show").[[32]](#cite_note-32) The band sold more than 70 million records worldwide, becoming the [fifth-best-selling boy band](/wiki/Boy_band#top "Boy band") in history.[[33]](#cite_note-nsync_records-33)

The rise of Timberlake's own stardom and the general decline in the popularity of boy bands led to the dissolution of NSYNC. Band member [Lance Bass](/wiki/Lance_Bass "Lance Bass") was openly critical of Timberlake's actions in his memoir *[Out of Sync](/wiki/Out_of_Sync_(book) "Out of Sync (book)")*.[[34]](#cite_note-34) By 2002, when the group went on an indefinite hiatus and members were doing individual projects, he partnered with [Pharrell Williams](/wiki/Pharrell_Williams "Pharrell Williams") of the producing team [The Neptunes](/wiki/The_Neptunes "The Neptunes")–and [Timbaland](/wiki/Timbaland "Timbaland") to start working on new music.[[35]](#cite_note-vanity_fair-35) The idea of going solo was strengthened a year earlier. Timberlake originally wrote the single "[Gone](/wiki/Gone_(NSYNC_song) "Gone (NSYNC song)")" around 2001 for [Michael Jackson](/wiki/Michael_Jackson "Michael Jackson"), but he turned the song down, and it was instead recorded by NSYNC. Before its release, however, Jackson contacted him. Timberlake declared in a later interview that the first time he felt confident to go solo happened after that conversation.[[36]](#cite_note-jackson-36) In a retrospective article in 2020, *[Billboard](/wiki/Billboard_(magazine) "Billboard (magazine)")* considered *Celebrity* "the group's swan song, setting the stage for Timberlake's equally massive solo career".[[37]](#cite_note-Greatest-37)

> [Michael] called me on the phone and said that he wanted to cut the record ("Gone"), but he wanted it to be a duet between himself and I. And I said, 'Well ... we've already cut the song as an 'N Sync record. Could we do, like, 'N Sync featuring Michael Jackson', or 'Michael Jackson featuring 'N Sync'? And he was very absolute about the fact that he wanted it to be a duet between himself and I.. [That was] the first time I ever really felt the confidence to do it

– Timberlake on *[Master Class](/wiki/Oprah%27s_Master_Class "Oprah's Master Class")*, 2014[[36]](#cite_note-jackson-36)

### 2002–2004: *Justified* and Super Bowl XXXVIII controversy

Main article: [Super Bowl XXXVIII halftime show controversy](/wiki/Super_Bowl_XXXVIII_halftime_show_controversy "Super Bowl XXXVIII halftime show controversy")

In August 2002, Timberlake performed at the [2002 MTV Video Music Awards](/wiki/2002_MTV_Video_Music_Awards "2002 MTV Video Music Awards"), where he premiered his debut solo single, "[Like I Love You](/wiki/Like_I_Love_You "Like I Love You")";[[38]](#cite_note-38) which peaked at number 11 on the [*Billboard* Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100")[[39]](#cite_note-timberlake_billboard-39) and number two on the [UK Singles Chart](/wiki/UK_Singles_Chart "UK Singles Chart").[[40]](#cite_note-40) His debut solo album, *[Justified](/wiki/Justified_(album) "Justified (album)"),* was released in November[[41]](#cite_note-41) and debuted at number two on the [*Billboard* 200](/wiki/Billboard_200 "Billboard 200") with first-week sales of 439,000 copies,[[42]](#cite_note-mtv4-42) fewer than previous 'N Sync releases. It sold over three million copies in the U.S. and more than ten million copies worldwide.[[43]](#cite_note-43) Its [R&B](/wiki/Contemporary_R%26B "Contemporary R&B") influence, provided by [hip-hop](/wiki/Hip-hop "Hip-hop") producers [The Neptunes](/wiki/The_Neptunes "The Neptunes") and [Timbaland](/wiki/Timbaland "Timbaland"),[[44]](#cite_note-44) was complimented by music critics. About the musical direction of the record, he commented, "I just want to do R&B. It's what I grew up listening to".[[45]](#cite_note-45) The album spawned the top-five singles "[Cry Me a River](/wiki/Cry_Me_a_River_(Justin_Timberlake_song) "Cry Me a River (Justin Timberlake song)")" and "[Rock Your Body](/wiki/Rock_Your_Body "Rock Your Body")".[[39]](#cite_note-timberlake_billboard-39)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/52/Justin_Timberlake_-_Justified_World_Tour_-_Earls_Court_-_6.jpg/250px-Justin_Timberlake_-_Justified_World_Tour_-_Earls_Court_-_6.jpg)](/wiki/File:Justin_Timberlake_-_Justified_World_Tour_-_Earls_Court_-_6.jpg)

Timberlake in London on his [Justified World Tour](/wiki/Justified_World_Tour "Justified World Tour"), December 2003

Near the end of 2002, Timberlake was the first celebrity to appear on *[Punk'd](/wiki/Punk%27d "Punk'd")*, a "candid camera" type show created by [Ashton Kutcher](/wiki/Ashton_Kutcher "Ashton Kutcher") to trick celebrities.[[46]](#cite_note-46) Timberlake, who cried during the episode, later admitted to being under the influence of [cannabis](/wiki/Cannabis_(drug) "Cannabis (drug)") when he was pranked.[[47]](#cite_note-47) Three episodes later, he set up [Kelly Osbourne](/wiki/Kelly_Osbourne "Kelly Osbourne") to be "punk'd", thus making him the first celebrity to appear on the show more than once.[[48]](#cite_note-48) Timberlake later spoofed [Ashton Kutcher](/wiki/Ashton_Kutcher "Ashton Kutcher") and *Punk'd* in a 2003 episode of NBC's *[Saturday Night Live](/wiki/Saturday_Night_Live "Saturday Night Live")*.[[49]](#cite_note-49) Timberlake co-starred in a skit titled *[The Barry Gibb Talk Show](/wiki/The_Barry_Gibb_Talk_Show "The Barry Gibb Talk Show")* alongside comedian [Jimmy Fallon](/wiki/Jimmy_Fallon "Jimmy Fallon"), where the duo portrayed [Bee Gees](/wiki/Bee_Gees "Bee Gees") brothers [Barry](/wiki/Barry_Gibb "Barry Gibb") and [Robin Gibb](/wiki/Robin_Gibb "Robin Gibb"). It marked the beginning of a long-running friendship and collaboration with Fallon.[[50]](#cite_note-jfallon-50)

In summer 2003, Timberlake and [Christina Aguilera](/wiki/Christina_Aguilera "Christina Aguilera") co-headlined [The Justified & Stripped Tour](/wiki/The_Justified_%26_Stripped_Tour "The Justified & Stripped Tour").[[51]](#cite_note-51) Later that year, he released the single "[I'm Lovin' It](/wiki/I%27m_Lovin%27_It_(song) "I'm Lovin' It (song)")", used by [McDonald's](/wiki/McDonald%27s "McDonald's") as the theme to its "I'm Lovin' It" campaign. The deal with McDonald's earned Timberlake an estimated $6 million.[[52]](#cite_note-p4k-52) A tour titled [Justified and Lovin' It Live](/wiki/Justified_and_Lovin%27_It_Live "Justified and Lovin' It Live") was included with the deal, following his initial [Justified World Tour](/wiki/Justified_World_Tour "Justified World Tour").[[53]](#cite_note-53) Timberlake was featured on [Nelly](/wiki/Nelly "Nelly")'s song, "[Work It](/wiki/Work_It_(Nelly_song) "Work It (Nelly song)")", which was [remixed](/wiki/Remix "Remix") and included on Nelly's 2003 remix album.[[54]](#cite_note-54) In October 2003, Timberlake made his first sole appearance on *[Saturday Night Live](/wiki/Saturday_Night_Live "Saturday Night Live")* both as host and musical guest, pairing with comedian [Jimmy Fallon](/wiki/Jimmy_Fallon "Jimmy Fallon") on the later-recurring skit *[The Barry Gibb Talk Show](/wiki/The_Barry_Gibb_Talk_Show "The Barry Gibb Talk Show")*.[[55]](#cite_note-revs_up-55)[[56]](#cite_note-Moss-56)

In February 2004, during the [Super Bowl XXXVIII halftime show](/wiki/Super_Bowl_XXXVIII_halftime_show "Super Bowl XXXVIII halftime show"), broadcast on the [CBS](/wiki/CBS "CBS") television network from [Houston](/wiki/Houston "Houston"), Timberlake performed with [Janet Jackson](/wiki/Janet_Jackson "Janet Jackson") before a television audience of more than 140 million viewers. At the end of the performance, as the song drew to a close, Timberlake tore off a part of Jackson's black leather costume in a "costume reveal" meant to accompany a portion of the song lyrics. Jackson's representative explained Timberlake intended "to pull away the rubber bustier to reveal a red lace bra. Part of the costume detached, and Jackson's breast was briefly exposed.[[57]](#cite_note-CNNCostumeReveal-57) Timberlake apologized for the incident, stating he was "sorry that anyone was offended by the wardrobe malfunction during the halftime performance of the Super Bowl..."[[57]](#cite_note-CNNCostumeReveal-57) The phrase "[wardrobe malfunction](/wiki/Wardrobe_malfunction "Wardrobe malfunction")" has since been used by the media to refer to the incident and has entered [pop culture](/wiki/Pop_culture "Pop culture").[[58]](#cite_note-58) Timberlake and Jackson were threatened with exclusion from the [2004 Grammy Awards](/wiki/2004_Grammy_Awards "2004 Grammy Awards") unless they agreed to apologize on screen at the event. Timberlake attended and issued a scripted apology when accepting the first of two [Grammy Awards](/wiki/Grammy_Award "Grammy Award") he received that night ([Best Pop Vocal Album](/wiki/Best_Pop_Vocal_Album "Best Pop Vocal Album") for *Justified* and [Best Male Pop Vocal Performance](/wiki/Best_Male_Pop_Vocal_Performance "Best Male Pop Vocal Performance") for "Cry Me a River").[[59]](#cite_note-:0-59) He had also been nominated for [Album of the Year](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Album_of_the_Year "Grammy Award for Album of the Year") for *Justified* and [Record of the Year](/wiki/Record_of_the_Year "Record of the Year") along with [Best Rap/Sung Collaboration](/wiki/Best_Rap/Sung_Collaboration "Best Rap/Sung Collaboration") for "[Where Is the Love?](/wiki/Where_Is_the_Love%3F "Where Is the Love?")" with [The Black Eyed Peas](/wiki/The_Black_Eyed_Peas "The Black Eyed Peas").[[59]](#cite_note-:0-59)

### 2004–2007: Films and *FutureSex/LoveSounds*

After the Super Bowl incident, Timberlake was "burned out" from his music career, opting out of an NSYNC reunion and elevating his acting career,[[55]](#cite_note-revs_up-55) having starred in a few feature films earlier in his career.[[13]](#cite_note-allmusicbio-13) The first role he took during this time was as a journalist in *[Edison Force](/wiki/Edison_Force "Edison Force")*, filmed in 2004 and released direct-to-video on July 18, 2006.[[60]](#cite_note-60) He also appeared in the films *[Alpha Dog](/wiki/Alpha_Dog "Alpha Dog")* (2006) with [Bruce Willis](/wiki/Bruce_Willis "Bruce Willis"),[[61]](#cite_note-61)[[62]](#cite_note-62) *[Black Snake Moan](/wiki/Black_Snake_Moan_(film) "Black Snake Moan (film)")* (2006) with [Samuel L. Jackson](/wiki/Samuel_L._Jackson "Samuel L. Jackson"),[[63]](#cite_note-63)[[64]](#cite_note-64) [Richard Kelly](/wiki/Richard_Kelly_(director) "Richard Kelly (director)")'s *[Southland Tales](/wiki/Southland_Tales "Southland Tales")* (2006),[[65]](#cite_note-goodbye-65) and voiced [Prince Artie Pendragon](/wiki/King_Arthur "King Arthur") in the animated film *[Shrek the Third](/wiki/Shrek_the_Third "Shrek the Third")* (2007).[[66]](#cite_note-66) Timberlake was considered to play the role of Roger Davis in the [film version](/wiki/Rent_(film) "Rent (film)") of the rock musical *[Rent](/wiki/Rent_(musical) "Rent (musical)")*, but director [Chris Columbus](/wiki/Chris_Columbus_(filmmaker) "Chris Columbus (filmmaker)") insisted that only the original [Broadway](/wiki/Broadway_theatre "Broadway theatre") members could convey the true meaning of *Rent*, so [Adam Pascal](/wiki/Adam_Pascal "Adam Pascal") reprised the role.[[67]](#cite_note-67)

During his hiatus from music, he continued to record with other artists. In 2004, he recorded "Good Foot" with Timbaland for the [soundtrack](/wiki/Shark_Tale#Soundtrack "Shark Tale") of *[Shark Tale](/wiki/Shark_Tale "Shark Tale")*.[[68]](#cite_note-janet-68) After "[Where Is the Love?](/wiki/Where_Is_the_Love%3F "Where Is the Love?")", he again collaborated with the Black Eyed Peas on the 2005 track "My Style" from their album *[Monkey Business](/wiki/Monkey_Business_(The_Black_Eyed_Peas_album) "Monkey Business (The Black Eyed Peas album)")*.[[69]](#cite_note-69) That year, he collaborated with [Charlie Wilson](/wiki/Charlie_Wilson_(singer) "Charlie Wilson (singer)") and Black Eyed Peas member [Will.i.am](/wiki/Will.i.am "Will.i.am") on the song "Floatin'" on the former's album *[Charlie, Last Name Wilson](/wiki/Charlie,_Last_Name_Wilson "Charlie, Last Name Wilson")*,[[70]](#cite_note-70) and collaborated on a track with Brazilian music artist [Sérgio Mendes](/wiki/S%C3%A9rgio_Mendes "Sérgio Mendes") the following year.[[71]](#cite_note-71) When recording the 2005 single "[Signs](/wiki/Signs_(Snoop_Dogg_song) "Signs (Snoop Dogg song)")" with [Snoop Dogg](/wiki/Snoop_Dogg "Snoop Dogg"), Timberlake discovered a throat condition.[[72]](#cite_note-72) [Nodules](/wiki/Nodule_(medicine) "Nodule (medicine)") were subsequently removed from his throat in an operation that took place on May 5, 2005.[[73]](#cite_note-73) He was advised not to sing or speak loudly for at least a few months.[[74]](#cite_note-74) In 2005, he began his own record company, [JayTee records](/wiki/JayTee_records "JayTee records").[[75]](#cite_note-75)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/26/Jtstpaul.jpg/250px-Jtstpaul.jpg)](/wiki/File:Jtstpaul.jpg)

Timberlake performing at a concert in [St. Paul, Minnesota](/wiki/Saint_Paul,_Minnesota "Saint Paul, Minnesota"), in January 2007 during the [FutureSex/LoveShow](/wiki/FutureSex/LoveShow "FutureSex/LoveShow")

> *FutureSex/LoveSounds*, a full-length LP of coherence and uniform song-to-song quality virtually unheard of in pop music since the '80s golden age of Michael, Madonna, Janet and Prince... Like one of his suit-and-vest combos, Timberlake himself continued to wear pop stardom exceptionally well. He glided through his music videos and live performances with the confidence of a man who'd already spent a large percentage of his life in the spotlight, and who never doubted he'd be able to return to music on top.

—*Billboard*'s "The Greatest Pop Star By Year (1981–2019)"[[37]](#cite_note-Greatest-37)

In December 2005, Timberlake began production on his second album, *[FutureSex/LoveSounds](/wiki/FutureSex/LoveSounds "FutureSex/LoveSounds")*, with returning producer Timbaland.[[55]](#cite_note-revs_up-55) Wanting an experimental direction, his goal for the album was "to capture moments' with a vivid, raw, off-the-cuff sound", while managing the recording sessions.[[76]](#cite_note-Moss_p_2-76)[[77]](#cite_note-Moss3-77) At the time, Timberlake developed an interest in a [rock](/wiki/Rock_music "Rock music") direction,[[55]](#cite_note-revs_up-55) drawing inspiration from [David Bowie](/wiki/David_Bowie "David Bowie") and [Prince](/wiki/Prince_(musician) "Prince (musician)"),[[78]](#cite_note-backsexy-78) and even took influence from [trance music](/wiki/Trance_music "Trance music") into the album.[[79]](#cite_note-HovaVibeStudio-79) In July 2006, the album's lead single, "[SexyBack](/wiki/SexyBack "SexyBack")", was released,[[80]](#cite_note-timb-80) and in a few weeks, it reached number one on the *Billboard* Hot 100, where it remained for seven consecutive weeks.[[81]](#cite_note-81)

Timberlake released his second album, *[FutureSex/LoveSounds](/wiki/FutureSex/LoveSounds "FutureSex/LoveSounds")*, on September 12, 2006. The album, which Timberlake created from 2005 to 2006, debuted at number one on the *Billboard* 200 album chart, selling 684,000 copies its first week.[[82]](#cite_note-82) It became the biggest album for pre-orders on [iTunes](/wiki/ITunes_Store "ITunes Store") and beat [Coldplay](/wiki/Coldplay "Coldplay")'s record for the biggest one-week sales of a digital album.[[83]](#cite_note-83) The album was produced by Timbaland and [Danja](/wiki/Danja_(record_producer) "Danja (record producer)") (who produced a bulk of the album), [will.i.am](/wiki/Will.i.am "Will.i.am"), [Rick Rubin](/wiki/Rick_Rubin "Rick Rubin"), and Timberlake himself, and features guest vocals by [Snoop Dogg](/wiki/Snoop_Dogg "Snoop Dogg"), [Three 6 Mafia](/wiki/Three_6_Mafia "Three 6 Mafia"), [T.I.](/wiki/T.I. "T.I."), and will.i.am.[[84]](#cite_note-84) A studio representative described it as being "all about sexiness" and aiming for "an adult feel".[[85]](#cite_note-85) "[My Love](/wiki/My_Love_(Justin_Timberlake_song) "My Love (Justin Timberlake song)")", the album's second single, also produced by Timbaland and featuring rapper [T.I.](/wiki/T.I. "T.I."),[[86]](#cite_note-norris-86) reached number one on the Hot 100,[[87]](#cite_note-87) as did its third single, "[What Goes Around... Comes Around](/wiki/What_Goes_Around..._Comes_Around "What Goes Around... Comes Around")".[[88]](#cite_note-88) The song is reported to have been inspired by the breakup of his childhood friend and business partner, Trace Ayala, with actress [Elisha Cuthbert](/wiki/Elisha_Cuthbert "Elisha Cuthbert").[[89]](#cite_note-89)

Timberlake hosted many music events, including the *[2006 European MTV Music Awards](/wiki/2006_MTV_Europe_Music_Awards "2006 MTV Europe Music Awards")*[[90]](#cite_note-90) and *[2007 Kids' Choice Awards](/wiki/2007_Kids%27_Choice_Awards "2007 Kids' Choice Awards")*.[[91]](#cite_note-91) On December 16, 2006, Timberlake hosted *[Saturday Night Live](/wiki/Saturday_Night_Live "Saturday Night Live")*, doing double duty as both host and musical guest for the second time. During this appearance, he and [Andy Samberg](/wiki/Andy_Samberg "Andy Samberg") performed an R&B song for a skit titled "[Dick in a Box](/wiki/Dick_in_a_Box "Dick in a Box")".[[92]](#cite_note-92) Called "one of the most iconic musical moments in the show's history" by *[Billboard](/wiki/Billboard_(magazine) "Billboard (magazine)")*, it became a viral hit and one of the most viewed videos on [YouTube](/wiki/YouTube "YouTube") at the time.[[93]](#cite_note-billboard.com-93) *[Rolling Stone](/wiki/Rolling_Stone "Rolling Stone")* listed the skit at number three on their "50 Greatest 'Saturday Night Live' Sketches of All-Time".[[94]](#cite_note-94) The song earned him an [Emmy Award](/wiki/Primetime_Emmy_Award_for_Outstanding_Original_Music_and_Lyrics "Primetime Emmy Award for Outstanding Original Music and Lyrics")[[95]](#cite_note-95) and was later featured on [The Lonely Island](/wiki/The_Lonely_Island "The Lonely Island")'s debut album, *[Incredibad](/wiki/Incredibad "Incredibad")*.[[96]](#cite_note-mtv-96)

In January 2007, Timberlake embarked on the [FutureSex/LoveShow](/wiki/FutureSex/LoveShow "FutureSex/LoveShow") tour.[[97]](#cite_note-97) Following singles off the album, "[LoveStoned/I Think She Knows (Interlude)](/wiki/LoveStoned "LoveStoned")" and "[Until the End of Time](/wiki/Until_the_End_of_Time_(Justin_Timberlake_and_Beyonc%C3%A9_song) "Until the End of Time (Justin Timberlake and Beyoncé song)")" peaked within the top 20 on the Hot 100,[[98]](#cite_note-chart-98) while the fourth single, "[Summer Love](/wiki/Summer_Love_(Justin_Timberlake_song) "Summer Love (Justin Timberlake song)")", reached the top 10.[[99]](#cite_note-99) The song "[Give It to Me](/wiki/Give_It_to_Me_(Timbaland_song) "Give It to Me (Timbaland song)")", a Timbaland single on which Timberlake guests with Nelly Furtado, reached the [Hot 100](/wiki/Hot_100 "Hot 100") number-one spot.[[100]](#cite_note-100) Eventually, *FutureSex/LoveSounds* was added to the [Rock and Roll Hall of Fame](/wiki/Rock_and_Roll_Hall_of_Fame "Rock and Roll Hall of Fame")'s musical library and archive.[[101]](#cite_note-101)

### 2008–2012: Musical hiatus and focus on acting

The song "[4 Minutes](/wiki/4_Minutes "4 Minutes")" was first played by [Timbaland](/wiki/Timbaland "Timbaland") at Philadelphia's Jingle Ball on December 17, 2007.[[102]](#cite_note-102) When released on March 17, 2008, "4 Minutes" was revealed to be a duet between Timberlake and [Madonna](/wiki/Madonna "Madonna"), with backing vocals by Timbaland. It was the lead single from Madonna's eleventh album, *[Hard Candy](/wiki/Hard_Candy_(Madonna_album) "Hard Candy (Madonna album)")*, which featured four other songwriting collaborations with Timberlake, who was also one of the executive producers.[[103]](#cite_note-103) The single was an international hit, topping the charts in over 21 countries worldwide.[[104]](#cite_note-104)

In June 2007, Timberlake co-wrote, produced, and provided vocals for the songs "Nite Runner" and "[Falling Down](/wiki/Falling_Down_(Duran_Duran_song) "Falling Down (Duran Duran song)")" for [Duran Duran](/wiki/Duran_Duran "Duran Duran")'s album *[Red Carpet Massacre](/wiki/Red_Carpet_Massacre "Red Carpet Massacre")*, released on November 13, 2007. "Falling Down" was released as a single in the UK on the previous day.[[105]](#cite_note-105) Also in 2007, Timberlake made an appearance on [50 Cent](/wiki/50_Cent "50 Cent")'s third album, *[Curtis](/wiki/Curtis_(50_Cent_album) "Curtis (50 Cent album)")*. Timberlake, along with Timbaland, is featured on a track called "[Ayo Technology](/wiki/Ayo_Technology "Ayo Technology")", which was the album's fourth single. Also, another possible collaboration was to occur with [Lil Wayne](/wiki/Lil_Wayne "Lil Wayne") for his album *[Tha Carter III](/wiki/Tha_Carter_III "Tha Carter III")* with [Nelly Furtado](/wiki/Nelly_Furtado "Nelly Furtado") and Timbaland.[[106]](#cite_note-106) With the wrapping up of the FutureSex/LoveSounds tour of Australasia and the Middle East in November 2007, Timberlake resumed his film career. Projects underway early in 2008 were starring roles in [Mike Myers](/wiki/Mike_Myers "Mike Myers")' comedy *[The Love Guru](/wiki/The_Love_Guru "The Love Guru")* (released June 20, 2008) and Mike Meredith's drama *[The Open Road](/wiki/The_Open_Road "The Open Road")* (released August 28, 2009). In March 2008, it was announced that he would be an executive producer in an American adaptation of the hit [Peruvian](/wiki/Peru "Peru") comedy *My Problem with Women* for [NBC](/wiki/NBC "NBC").[[107]](#cite_note-107) On November 20, 2008, *[TV Guide](/wiki/TV_Guide "TV Guide")* reported that Timberlake's next single, "Follow My Lead", which also featured vocals by Timberlake's protégée, former YouTube star [Esmée Denters](/wiki/Esm%C3%A9e_Denters "Esmée Denters"), would be available for exclusive download through [Myspace](/wiki/Myspace "Myspace"). All proceeds would go to [Shriners Hospitals for Children](/wiki/Shriners_Hospitals_for_Children "Shriners Hospitals for Children"), a charity dedicated to improving pediatric care for sick children.[[108]](#cite_note-108) In February 2008, Timberlake was awarded two [Grammy Awards](/wiki/Grammy_Award "Grammy Award"). At the [50th Grammy Awards](/wiki/50th_Grammy_Awards "50th Grammy Awards") ceremony, he won the [Award for Best Male Pop Vocal Performance](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_Male_Pop_Vocal_Performance "Grammy Award for Best Male Pop Vocal Performance") for "[What Goes Around...Comes Around](/wiki/What_Goes_Around...Comes_Around "What Goes Around...Comes Around")", and the [Award for Best Dance Recording](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_Dance_Recording "Grammy Award for Best Dance Recording") for "[LoveStoned/I Think She Knows](/wiki/LoveStoned/I_Think_She_Knows "LoveStoned/I Think She Knows")".[[109]](#cite_note-109)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c0/Justin_Timberlake_-_Andrew_Garfield_-_La_red_social_-_Madrid.jpg/250px-Justin_Timberlake_-_Andrew_Garfield_-_La_red_social_-_Madrid.jpg)](/wiki/File:Justin_Timberlake_-_Andrew_Garfield_-_La_red_social_-_Madrid.jpg)

Timberlake (left) with [Andrew Garfield](/wiki/Andrew_Garfield "Andrew Garfield") (right) at an event for *[The Social Network](/wiki/The_Social_Network "The Social Network")* in Madrid, October 2010

In 2008, a collaboration between Timberlake and [T.I.](/wiki/T.I. "T.I."), "[Dead and Gone](/wiki/Dead_and_Gone "Dead and Gone")", was featured on T.I.'s sixth album, *[Paper Trail](/wiki/Paper_Trail "Paper Trail")*, and was released as its fourth single late in 2009. In November 2008, it was confirmed that Timberlake would make a guest appearance and produce some tracks on [R&B](/wiki/Contemporary_R%26B "Contemporary R&B")/[pop](/wiki/Pop_music "Pop music") singer [Ciara](/wiki/Ciara "Ciara")'s upcoming album *[Fantasy Ride](/wiki/Fantasy_Ride "Fantasy Ride"),* due out May 5, 2009. Timberlake featured on Ciara's second single, "[Love Sex Magic](/wiki/Love_Sex_Magic "Love Sex Magic")", the video being shot on February 20, 2009.[[110]](#cite_note-110) The single became a worldwide hit, reaching the top ten in numerous countries and peaking at number one in several countries, including Taiwan, India, and Turkey. The song was nominated for [Best Pop Collaboration with Vocals](/wiki/Best_Pop_Collaboration_with_Vocals "Best Pop Collaboration with Vocals") at the [52nd Grammy Awards](/wiki/52nd_Grammy_Awards "52nd Grammy Awards"). Timberlake hosted the 16th [ESPY Awards](/wiki/ESPY_Awards "ESPY Awards"), and the musical number "I Love Sports" was later nominated for an Emmy Award.[[111]](#cite_note-111) Timberlake and his production team, [The Y's](/wiki/The_Y%27s "The Y's"), along with [Mike Elizondo](/wiki/Mike_Elizondo "Mike Elizondo"), produced and co-wrote the song "Don't Let Me Down" for [Leona Lewis](/wiki/Leona_Lewis "Leona Lewis")'s second album, *[Echo](/wiki/Echo_(Leona_Lewis_album) "Echo (Leona Lewis album)")*, released on November 17, 2009. Timberlake also co-wrote and performed on "[Carry Out](/wiki/Carry_Out "Carry Out")", the third single from Timbaland's album *[Shock Value II](/wiki/Shock_Value_II "Shock Value II")*, released on December 1, 2009.[[112]](#cite_note-112) Timberlake appeared on [Jimmy Fallon](/wiki/Jimmy_Fallon "Jimmy Fallon")'s debut as host of *[Late Night with Jimmy Fallon](/wiki/Late_Night_with_Jimmy_Fallon "Late Night with Jimmy Fallon")* on March 2, 2009.[[113]](#cite_note-113) Timberlake was the executive producer on the [MTV](/wiki/MTV "MTV") reality series *[The Phone](/wiki/The_Phone_(U.S._TV_series) "The Phone (U.S. TV series)")*, which premiered on April 21, 2009. In late 2009, Barbadian singer [Rihanna](/wiki/Rihanna "Rihanna") released the album *[Rated R](/wiki/Rated_R_(Rihanna_album) "Rated R (Rihanna album)")*, with Timberlake being one of the writers and producers.[[114]](#cite_note-114) They both worked together previously for her third album, *[Good Girl Gone Bad](/wiki/Good_Girl_Gone_Bad "Good Girl Gone Bad")*.[[115]](#cite_note-115)

From 2010, Timberlake increased his acting work. He played [Sean Parker](/wiki/Sean_Parker "Sean Parker"), the founder of [Napster](/wiki/Napster "Napster"), in the acclaimed film *[The Social Network](/wiki/The_Social_Network "The Social Network")* (2010),[[116]](#cite_note-116) and voiced [Boo-Boo Bear](/wiki/Boo-Boo_Bear "Boo-Boo Bear") in the live-action/animated comedy film *[Yogi Bear](/wiki/Yogi_Bear_(film) "Yogi Bear (film)")*, adapted from the [Hanna-Barbara series](/wiki/The_Yogi_Bear_Show "The Yogi Bear Show").[[117]](#cite_note-117) He also appeared at the 2010 MTV VMAs on September 12, 2010.[[118]](#cite_note-118) In 2011, he starred alongside [Cameron Diaz](/wiki/Cameron_Diaz "Cameron Diaz") in *[Bad Teacher](/wiki/Bad_Teacher "Bad Teacher")* and alongside [Mila Kunis](/wiki/Mila_Kunis "Mila Kunis") in *[Friends with Benefits](/wiki/Friends_with_Benefits_(film) "Friends with Benefits (film)")* and played Will Salas, the protagonist of *[In Time](/wiki/In_Time "In Time")*, a science fiction film by [Andrew Niccol](/wiki/Andrew_Niccol "Andrew Niccol").[[119]](#cite_note-119) He provided a feature and appeared in the music video for the song "[Motherlover](/wiki/Motherlover "Motherlover")" from [The Lonely Island](/wiki/The_Lonely_Island "The Lonely Island")'s second album, *[Turtleneck & Chain](/wiki/Turtleneck_%26_Chain "Turtleneck & Chain")*,[[120]](#cite_note-120) and directed and made a cameo in the [FreeSol](/wiki/FreeSol "FreeSol") music video "Hoodies On, Hats Low", which was released in August 2011.[[121]](#cite_note-121) The *Late Night with Jimmy Fallon* sketch "[History of Rap](/wiki/History_of_Rap "History of Rap")" was performed for the first time by both Fallon and Timberlake in 2010.[[122]](#cite_note-NYM-122) In July 2011, United States Marine Kelsey De Santis uploaded a [YouTube](/wiki/YouTube "YouTube") video asking Timberlake to be her date to the United States Marine Corps birthday ball; they attended the event on November 13, 2011, in [Richmond, Virginia](/wiki/Richmond,_Virginia "Richmond, Virginia").[[123]](#cite_note-123) His fifth *[Saturday Night Live](/wiki/Saturday_Night_Live "Saturday Night Live")* episode, as host and musical guest, was the most-watched episode in 14 months, with [Charles Barkley](/wiki/Charles_Barkley "Charles Barkley") as host and [Kelly Clarkson](/wiki/Kelly_Clarkson "Kelly Clarkson") as musical guest.[[124]](#cite_note-124)

### 2013–2017: *The 20/20 Experience*, *2 of 2*, and *Trolls*

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b5/Justin_Timberlake_2014_February_3.jpg/250px-Justin_Timberlake_2014_February_3.jpg)](/wiki/File:Justin_Timberlake_2014_February_3.jpg)

Timberlake performing during [The 20/20 Experience World Tour](/wiki/The_20/20_Experience_World_Tour "The 20/20 Experience World Tour"), February 2014. It is Timberlake's highest-grossing tour

Timberlake began working on his third album, *[The 20/20 Experience](/wiki/The_20/20_Experience "The 20/20 Experience")*, in June 2012 with "no rules and/or end goal in mind".[[125]](#cite_note-125) He publicly announced his return to the music industry in January 2013,[[126]](#cite_note-126) releasing the album's lead single, "[Suit & Tie](/wiki/Suit_%26_Tie "Suit & Tie")" featuring [Jay-Z](/wiki/Jay-Z "Jay-Z") later that month, which would eventually peak at number three on the *Billboard* Hot 100. After four years of not performing in concert, Timberlake appeared the night before the [2013 Super Bowl](/wiki/2013_Super_Bowl "2013 Super Bowl") and performed during the "[DirecTV](/wiki/DirecTV "DirecTV") Super Saturday Night" on February 2, 2013, in [New Orleans](/wiki/New_Orleans "New Orleans").[[127]](#cite_note-127) On February 10, 2013, he performed "Suit & Tie" with sepia-toned lighting at the [55th Annual Grammy Awards](/wiki/55th_Annual_Grammy_Awards "55th Annual Grammy Awards"), with Jay-Z joining him from the audience. On February 11, 2013, "[Mirrors](/wiki/Mirrors_(Justin_Timberlake_song) "Mirrors (Justin Timberlake song)")" was released as the second single from *The 20/20 Experience*.[[128]](#cite_note-128) The song would eventually peak at number two on the *Billboard* Hot 100 and number one on the [UK Singles Chart](/wiki/UK_Singles_Chart "UK Singles Chart"). *The 20/20 Experience* was released on March 19, 2013,[[129]](#cite_note-129) through [RCA Records](/wiki/RCA_Records "RCA Records") due to the disbandment of [Jive Records](/wiki/Jive_Records "Jive Records").[[130]](#cite_note-130)[[131]](#cite_note-131) The album set a digital sales record for being the fastest-selling album on the [iTunes Store](/wiki/ITunes_Store "ITunes Store")[[132]](#cite_note-132) and debuted at number one on the charts by moving just over 968,000 copies in the U.S., the biggest sales week of 2013.[[133]](#cite_note-133) It eventually became the best-selling album of the year in the country.[[134]](#cite_note-134)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/70/Justin_Timberlake_and_Barack_Obama_at_The_White_House_-_2.jpg/250px-Justin_Timberlake_and_Barack_Obama_at_The_White_House_-_2.jpg)](/wiki/File:Justin_Timberlake_and_Barack_Obama_at_The_White_House_-_2.jpg)

Timberlake and President Obama, 2013

Timberlake performed at the "In Performance at the White House: Memphis Soul" concert, held in the [East Room](/wiki/East_Room "East Room") of the [White House](/wiki/White_House "White House") and hosted by President [Barack Obama](/wiki/Barack_Obama "Barack Obama"), celebrating [Memphis soul](/wiki/Memphis_soul "Memphis soul") music from the 1960s.[[135]](#cite_note-135) Afterward, Timberlake officially announced [The 20/20 Experience World Tour](/wiki/The_20/20_Experience_World_Tour "The 20/20 Experience World Tour"), following his and Jay-Z's co-headlining concert tour, [Legends of the Summer Stadium Tour](/wiki/Legends_of_the_Summer_Stadium_Tour "Legends of the Summer Stadium Tour").[[136]](#cite_note-136) Timberlake also appeared on Jay-Z's twelfth album, *[Magna Carta... Holy Grail](/wiki/Magna_Carta..._Holy_Grail "Magna Carta... Holy Grail")* on three songs: "[Holy Grail](/wiki/Holy_Grail_(Jay-Z_song) "Holy Grail (Jay-Z song)")", "BBC" (along with [Nas](/wiki/Nas "Nas"), [Swizz Beatz](/wiki/Swizz_Beatz "Swizz Beatz"), Timbaland, [Pharrell Williams](/wiki/Pharrell_Williams "Pharrell Williams"), Niigo & [Beyoncé](/wiki/Beyonc%C3%A9 "Beyoncé")), and "[Heaven](/wiki/Heaven_(Jay-Z_song) "Heaven (Jay-Z song)")".[[137]](#cite_note-137) On August 25, 2013, Timberlake received the [Michael Jackson Video Vanguard Award](/wiki/Michael_Jackson_Video_Vanguard_Award "Michael Jackson Video Vanguard Award") at the [2013 MTV Video Music Awards](/wiki/2013_MTV_Video_Music_Awards "2013 MTV Video Music Awards"). He also took home three competitive awards, including [Video of the Year](/wiki/MTV_Video_Music_Award_for_Video_of_the_Year "MTV Video Music Award for Video of the Year") for "Mirrors".[[138]](#cite_note-vanguard-138) During his performance, Timberlake briefly reunited with his fellow former NSYNC bandmates for a medley of their hit songs "Girlfriend" and "Bye Bye Bye".[[139]](#cite_note-139)

Timberlake's fourth album, *[The 20/20 Experience – 2 of 2](/wiki/The_20/20_Experience_%E2%80%93_2_of_2 "The 20/20 Experience – 2 of 2")*, was released on September 30, 2013,[[140]](#cite_note-140) and debuted at number one on the *Billboard* 200.[[141]](#cite_note-141) Its lead single, "[Take Back the Night](/wiki/Take_Back_the_Night_(song) "Take Back the Night (song)")", was released on July 12, 2013,[[142]](#cite_note-142) followed by the second single, "[TKO](/wiki/TKO_(song) "TKO (song)")".[[143]](#cite_note-143) Timberlake was given a production and writing credit on [track six](/wiki/Partition_(song) "Partition (song)") on [Beyoncé](/wiki/Beyonc%C3%A9 "Beyoncé")'s [self-titled fifth album](/wiki/Beyonc%C3%A9_(album) "Beyoncé (album)"), which was released in December 2013.[[144]](#cite_note-144) At the [57th Annual Grammy Awards](/wiki/57th_Annual_Grammy_Awards "57th Annual Grammy Awards"), Timberlake was nominated for seven awards, eventually winning three: [Best R&B Song](/wiki/Best_R%26B_Song "Best R&B Song") for "[Pusher Love Girl](/wiki/Pusher_Love_Girl "Pusher Love Girl")", [Best Rap/Sung Collaboration](/wiki/Best_Rap/Sung_Collaboration "Best Rap/Sung Collaboration") for "[Holy Grail](/wiki/Holy_Grail_(Jay-Z_song) "Holy Grail (Jay-Z song)")", and [Best Music Video](/wiki/Best_Music_Video "Best Music Video") for "Suit & Tie", which was directed by *The Social Network* director [David Fincher](/wiki/David_Fincher "David Fincher").[[145]](#cite_note-145) On February 25, 2014, "[Not a Bad Thing](/wiki/Not_a_Bad_Thing "Not a Bad Thing")" was released as the third single from *The 20/20 Experience – 2 of 2*.[[146]](#cite_note-146) The song reached the top 10 on the Hot 100 and topped the [Mainstream Top 40](/wiki/Mainstream_Top_40 "Mainstream Top 40") chart.[[147]](#cite_note-147) In 2014, Timberlake appeared on [Michael Jackson](/wiki/Michael_Jackson "Michael Jackson")'s second posthumous record, *[Xscape](/wiki/Xscape_(album) "Xscape (album)")*, on the song "[Love Never Felt So Good](/wiki/Love_Never_Felt_So_Good "Love Never Felt So Good")", which was produced by Timbaland, [Jerome "J-Roc" Harmon](/wiki/Jerome_%22J-Roc%22_Harmon "Jerome \"J-Roc\" Harmon"), and Timberlake.[[148]](#cite_note-148) On May 14, 2014, a music video was also released featuring clips of Jackson, with Timberlake accompanied by several of Jackson's fans performing some of Jackson's signature moves. The video was directed by Timberlake and [Rich Lee](/wiki/Rich_Lee "Rich Lee").[[149]](#cite_note-149) During 2015, Timberlake performed along with [Jimmy Fallon](/wiki/Jimmy_Fallon "Jimmy Fallon") in the [*Saturday Night Live* 40th Anniversary](/wiki/Saturday_Night_Live_40th_Anniversary_Special "Saturday Night Live 40th Anniversary Special")'s cold open[[150]](#cite_note-150) and returned to *[The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon](/wiki/The_Tonight_Show_Starring_Jimmy_Fallon "The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon")* as a guest to perform a sixth edition of the sketch "[History of Rap](/wiki/History_of_Rap "History of Rap")".[[151]](#cite_note-151) He also performed along with [Chris Stapleton](/wiki/Chris_Stapleton "Chris Stapleton") at the [49th Annual Country Music Association Awards](/wiki/49th_Annual_Country_Music_Association_Awards "49th Annual Country Music Association Awards").[[152]](#cite_note-152)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a3/Justin_Timberlake_-_Cannes_2016_-_1.jpg/250px-Justin_Timberlake_-_Cannes_2016_-_1.jpg)](/wiki/File:Justin_Timberlake_-_Cannes_2016_-_1.jpg)

Timberlake at the [2016 Cannes Film Festival](/wiki/2016_Cannes_Film_Festival "2016 Cannes Film Festival")

Showcasing the final date of [The 20/20 Experience World Tour](/wiki/The_20/20_Experience_World_Tour "The 20/20 Experience World Tour") at Las Vegas' [MGM Grand Garden Arena](/wiki/MGM_Grand_Garden_Arena "MGM Grand Garden Arena"), the space-age-themed concert film titled *[Justin Timberlake + The Tennessee Kids](/wiki/Justin_Timberlake_%2B_The_Tennessee_Kids "Justin Timberlake + The Tennessee Kids")* and directed by [Jonathan Demme](/wiki/Jonathan_Demme "Jonathan Demme") premiered at the [2016 Toronto International Film Festival](/wiki/2016_Toronto_International_Film_Festival "2016 Toronto International Film Festival") on September 13.[[153]](#cite_note-153)[[154]](#cite_note-154) Timberlake dedicated the film to [Prince](/wiki/Prince_(musician) "Prince (musician)") for influencing his music.[[155]](#cite_note-155) Ahead of its debut at the film festival, the streaming service [Netflix](/wiki/Netflix "Netflix") released it on October 12.[[156]](#cite_note-156) Timberlake composed the soundtrack and served as music supervisor for the 2017 film *[The Book of Love](/wiki/The_Book_of_Love_(film) "The Book of Love (film)")*, which his wife, [Jessica Biel](/wiki/Jessica_Biel "Jessica Biel"), produced and starred in.[[157]](#cite_note-157)[[158]](#cite_note-158)

Timberlake voiced the lead character in [DreamWorks Animation](/wiki/DreamWorks_Animation "DreamWorks Animation")'s musical comedy *[Trolls](/wiki/Trolls_(film) "Trolls (film)")* opposite [Anna Kendrick](/wiki/Anna_Kendrick "Anna Kendrick"). The film was released in November 2016[[159]](#cite_note-159) and returned for its next two entries, *[Trolls: World Tour](/wiki/Trolls:_World_Tour "Trolls: World Tour")* and *[Trolls: Band Together](/wiki/Trolls_Band_Together "Trolls Band Together")*, in 2020 and 2023, respectively.[[160]](#cite_note-160) He also served as the executive music producer, performing [original music for the film](/wiki/Trolls_(soundtrack) "Trolls (soundtrack)").[[161]](#cite_note-161) The lead single, "[Can't Stop the Feeling!](/wiki/Can%27t_Stop_the_Feeling! "Can't Stop the Feeling!")", was released on May 6, 2016.[[162]](#cite_note-162) Timberlake was invited by Swedish broadcaster [Sveriges Television](/wiki/Sveriges_Television "Sveriges Television") (SVT) to perform "Can't Stop the Feeling" live during the interval act at the grand finale of the [Eurovision Song Contest 2016](/wiki/Eurovision_Song_Contest_2016 "Eurovision Song Contest 2016") on May 14, 2016.[[163]](#cite_note-163)[[164]](#cite_note-164) The single debuted at number one on the U.S. [*Billboard* Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100")[[165]](#cite_note-hot100numberone-165) and reached the top spot in 16 other countries. It became his eighth U.S. [Mainstream Top 40](/wiki/Mainstream_Top_40 "Mainstream Top 40") number-one song.[[166]](#cite_note-166) It became the [best-selling song](/wiki/List_of_best-selling_singles_in_the_United_States "List of best-selling singles in the United States") of the year in the U.S. with 2.4 million downloads sold.[[167]](#cite_note-167) On February 26, 2017, Timberlake opened the [89th Academy Awards](/wiki/89th_Academy_Awards "89th Academy Awards") with a performance of "Can't Stop the Feeling!" since the song earned him a nomination.[[168]](#cite_note-168) Also that year, Timberlake starred with [Kate Winslet](/wiki/Kate_Winslet "Kate Winslet") and [Juno Temple](/wiki/Juno_Temple "Juno Temple") in [Woody Allen](/wiki/Woody_Allen "Woody Allen")'s drama film *[Wonder Wheel](/wiki/Wonder_Wheel_(film) "Wonder Wheel (film)")*.[[169]](#cite_note-169) He headlined several festivals and live sets, including [Rock in Rio](/wiki/Rock_in_Rio "Rock in Rio"),[[170]](#cite_note-170) the [United States Grand Prix](/wiki/United_States_Grand_Prix "United States Grand Prix"),[[171]](#cite_note-171) and the [Pilgrimage Music & Cultural Festival](/wiki/Pilgrimage_Music_%26_Cultural_Festival "Pilgrimage Music & Cultural Festival"), which he co-produced.[[172]](#cite_note-172)

### 2018–2022: *Man of the Woods* and Super Bowl LII halftime show

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f6/Man_of_the_Woods_Tour_-_Miami_-_03.jpg/330px-Man_of_the_Woods_Tour_-_Miami_-_03.jpg)](/wiki/File:Man_of_the_Woods_Tour_-_Miami_-_03.jpg)

Timberlake performing on [The Man of the Woods Tour](/wiki/The_Man_of_the_Woods_Tour "The Man of the Woods Tour") in Miami on May 18, 2018, which was the sixth highest-grossing tour of 2018

> For the biggest pop stars in the world, the place where they have the most trouble is honesty... Because there's a formulaic sort of vulnerability... But if you're able to really screenshot your own vulnerability, frame it properly, and color-correct it, then it becomes something that every human can relate to. And I think Justin is in the place where he's mastering that right now.

– Pharrell Williams on the recording sessions for *[Man of the Woods](/wiki/Man_of_the_Woods "Man of the Woods")*, Timberlake's fifth album[[173]](#cite_note-variety-173)

Speaking of his fifth album in 2016, Timberlake stated, "I think where I grew up in America has a lot of influence. Growing up in Tennessee—very central of the country—Memphis is known as the birthplace of rock & roll, but also the home of the blues, but Nashville's right down the street so there's a lot of country music."[[174]](#cite_note-174) In following interviews, Timberlake confirmed working with producers [Timbaland](/wiki/Timbaland "Timbaland") and [Pharrell Williams](/wiki/Pharrell_Williams "Pharrell Williams")[[175]](#cite_note-175) while stating, "It sounds more like where I've come from than any other music I've ever made... It's Southern American music. But I want to make it sound modern – at least that's the idea right now."[[176]](#cite_note-176)

His fifth album, *[Man of the Woods](/wiki/Man_of_the_Woods "Man of the Woods")*, was released on February 2, 2018,[[177]](#cite_note-177) two days before he headlined the [Super Bowl LII halftime show](/wiki/Super_Bowl_LII_halftime_show "Super Bowl LII halftime show") in [Minneapolis](/wiki/Minneapolis "Minneapolis"), [Minnesota](/wiki/Minnesota "Minnesota"), on February 4. Timberlake performed a medley of his songs featuring both the Tennessee Kids and the [Minnesota Marching Band](/wiki/Minnesota_Marching_Band "Minnesota Marching Band") alongside him, as well as a duet of "[I Would Die 4 U](/wiki/I_Would_Die_4_U "I Would Die 4 U")" with a video projection of late singer [Prince](/wiki/Prince_(musician) "Prince (musician)") on screen.[[178]](#cite_note-178) The show contained multiple selections from *Man of the Woods,* which is named after his son Silas, whose name means "from the forest".[[179]](#cite_note-179) The album's first single, "[Filthy](/wiki/Filthy_(song) "Filthy (song)")", was released on January 5, along with its accompanying video directed by [Mark Romanek](/wiki/Mark_Romanek "Mark Romanek").[[180]](#cite_note-180) The [electro-funk](/wiki/Electro-funk "Electro-funk") song[[181]](#cite_note-181) has reached number nine in the U.S.[[182]](#cite_note-182) and number five in Canada.[[183]](#cite_note-CanadaHot100-183) The songs "[Supplies](/wiki/Supplies_(song) "Supplies (song)")", "[Say Something](/wiki/Say_Something_(Justin_Timberlake_song) "Say Something (Justin Timberlake song)")" featuring [Chris Stapleton](/wiki/Chris_Stapleton "Chris Stapleton"), and "[Man of the Woods](/wiki/Man_of_the_Woods_(song) "Man of the Woods (song)")" were also released along with music videos ahead of the album.[[184]](#cite_note-184) His collaboration with Stapleton also reached the top 10 in the U.S. and Canada.[[183]](#cite_note-CanadaHot100-183)[[185]](#cite_note-185)

*Man of the Woods* topped the *Billboard* 200 with the biggest first week sales of the year at the time, selling 293,000 total units.[[186]](#cite_note-186) *Man of the Woods* also marks Timberlake's fourth consecutive No. 1 album and has since been certified [Platinum](/wiki/Music_recording_sales_certification "Music recording sales certification") by the Recording Industry Association of America (RIAA).[[187]](#cite_note-187) *Man of the Woods* concluded 2018 as the sixth best-selling album of the year.[[188]](#cite_note-Scorpion-188) An accompanying [tour of the same name](/wiki/Man_of_the_Woods_Tour "Man of the Woods Tour") began on March 13, 2018, in [Toronto](/wiki/Toronto "Toronto"), Canada and concluded on April 13, 2019, in [Uncasville](/wiki/Uncasville "Uncasville").[[189]](#cite_note-189) *The Man of the Woods Tour* was the sixth-highest-grossing tour of 2018.[[190]](#cite_note-gross-190)

In May 2019, Timberlake received an honorary [doctorate](/wiki/Doctorate "Doctorate") from [Berklee College of Music](/wiki/Berklee_College_of_Music "Berklee College of Music").[[191]](#cite_note-Honorary-191) [Missy Elliott](/wiki/Missy_Elliott "Missy Elliott") and [Alex Lacamoire](/wiki/Alex_Lacamoire "Alex Lacamoire") also received doctorates at the ceremony.[[192]](#cite_note-192)

Following the announcement of *[Trolls World Tour](/wiki/Trolls_World_Tour "Trolls World Tour")*, Timberlake teased potential collaborations with a number of artists through a series of Instagram posts. This included frequent collaborators [Pharrell Williams](/wiki/Pharrell_Williams "Pharrell Williams"), Nathaniel Hills, and [Rob Knox](/wiki/Rob_Knox_(producer) "Rob Knox (producer)"), as well as new collaborators such as [Anderson .Paak](/wiki/Anderson_.Paak "Anderson .Paak"), [Brandy Norwood](/wiki/Brandy_Norwood "Brandy Norwood"), [Lizzo](/wiki/Lizzo "Lizzo"), and [Meek Mill](/wiki/Meek_Mill "Meek Mill").[[193]](#cite_note-193)[[194]](#cite_note-194)[[195]](#cite_note-195)[[196]](#cite_note-196) On February 26, 2020, [SZA](/wiki/SZA "SZA") and Timberlake released the single "[The Other Side](/wiki/The_Other_Side_(SZA_and_Justin_Timberlake_song) "The Other Side (SZA and Justin Timberlake song)")", a song part of the *[Trolls World Tour](/wiki/Trolls_World_Tour "Trolls World Tour")* soundtrack, alongside its music video.[[197]](#cite_note-197)[[198]](#cite_note-198)[[199]](#cite_note-199) On March 9, 2020, Timberlake released a teaser for his upcoming song "Don't Slack", which features [Anderson .Paak](/wiki/Anderson_.Paak "Anderson .Paak"), and is also part of the *[Trolls World Tour](/wiki/Trolls_World_Tour "Trolls World Tour")* soundtrack. The song was released on March 10, 2020.[[200]](#cite_note-200)[[201]](#cite_note-201)

In September 2020, Timberlake and producer Timbaland teased a potential upcoming collaboration with Canadian musician [Justin Bieber](/wiki/Justin_Bieber "Justin Bieber"), as well as collaborations with [Justine Skye](/wiki/Justine_Skye "Justine Skye"), [Hit-Boy](/wiki/Hit-Boy "Hit-Boy"), and [Ty Dolla Sign](/wiki/Ty_Dolla_Sign "Ty Dolla Sign").[[202]](#cite_note-202)[[203]](#cite_note-203) In December 2020, Timberlake and [Ant Clemons](/wiki/Ant_Clemons "Ant Clemons") released the single "[Better Days](/wiki/Better_Days_(Ant_Clemons_song) "Better Days (Ant Clemons song)")", which premiered on the Rock the Runoff virtual concert, held by [Stacey Abrams](/wiki/Stacey_Abrams "Stacey Abrams")' organization Fair Fight.[[204]](#cite_note-204)

In January 2021, Timberlake performed as part of the [inauguration of Joe Biden](/wiki/Inauguration_of_Joe_Biden "Inauguration of Joe Biden").[[205]](#cite_note-205)[[206]](#cite_note-206) Performing from his hometown of Memphis, Timberlake performed his collaboration with Ant Clemons during the *[Celebrating America](/wiki/Celebrating_America "Celebrating America")* special.[[207]](#cite_note-207) Timberlake performed at Pharrell Williams' Something in the Water festival in Washington, D.C., where he was joined on stage by [T.I.](/wiki/T.I. "T.I.") and [Clipse](/wiki/Clipse "Clipse") for his five-song set.[[208]](#cite_note-208)

### 2023–present: *Everything I Thought It Was*

In May 2023, Timbaland revealed that Timberlake's sixth album was completed, adding that the pair had revived the sound from the *FutureSex/LoveSounds* era.[[209]](#cite_note-209) On September 1, 2023, Timbaland released the single "[Keep Going Up](/wiki/Keep_Going_Up "Keep Going Up")", in collaboration with Furtado and Timberlake. Timberlake also reunited with his fellow former band members of [NSYNC](/wiki/NSYNC "NSYNC") later that month and released the single "[Better Place](/wiki/Better_Place_(NSYNC_song) "Better Place (NSYNC song)")" to support the [*Trolls Band Together* soundtrack](/wiki/Trolls_Band_Together_(soundtrack) "Trolls Band Together (soundtrack)").[[210]](#cite_note-et-210)[[211]](#cite_note-211)[[212]](#cite_note-212)[[213]](#cite_note-213) In addition to "Keep Going Up" and "Better Place", Timberlake had also collaborated with [Meek Mill](/wiki/Meek_Mill "Meek Mill") on the song [Believe](/wiki/Believe_(Meek_Mill_song) "Believe (Meek Mill song)"), [Romeo Santos](/wiki/Romeo_Santos "Romeo Santos") on the song "[Sin Fin](/wiki/Sin_Fin "Sin Fin")",[[214]](#cite_note-214) [DJ Khaled](/wiki/DJ_Khaled "DJ Khaled") on his album *[Khaled Khaled](/wiki/Khaled_Khaled "Khaled Khaled")* on the song "Just Be", [Justine Skye](/wiki/Justine_Skye "Justine Skye") on her album *Space & Time* on the song "Innocent" [Jack Harlow](/wiki/Jack_Harlow "Jack Harlow") on his album *[Come Home the Kids Miss You](/wiki/Come_Home_the_Kids_Miss_You "Come Home the Kids Miss You")* on the song "Parent Trap"; a remix of [Coco Jones](/wiki/Coco_Jones "Coco Jones")' "[ICU](/wiki/ICU_(song)#Remix "ICU (song)")"[[215]](#cite_note-remix-215) and a further remix of the song "[3D](/wiki/3D_(song) "3D (song)")" by [Jungkook](/wiki/Jungkook "Jungkook").[[216]](#cite_note-Forbes-216) Timberlake had also worked with [Calvin Harris](/wiki/Calvin_Harris "Calvin Harris"), producer of "[Fuckin' Up the Disco](/wiki/Fuckin%27_Up_the_Disco "Fuckin' Up the Disco")" and "[No Angels](/wiki/No_Angels_(Justin_Timberlake_song) "No Angels (Justin Timberlake song)")", on his song "[Stay with Me](/wiki/Stay_with_Me_(Calvin_Harris,_Justin_Timberlake,_Halsey_and_Pharrell_Williams_song) "Stay with Me (Calvin Harris, Justin Timberlake, Halsey and Pharrell Williams song)")", alongside [Pharrell Williams](/wiki/Pharrell_Williams "Pharrell Williams") and [Halsey](/wiki/Halsey_(singer) "Halsey (singer)").[[217]](#cite_note-217)[[218]](#cite_note-218)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/3d/Justin_Timberlake_Performing_in_Seattle%2C_Washington.jpg/250px-Justin_Timberlake_Performing_in_Seattle%2C_Washington.jpg)](/wiki/File:Justin_Timberlake_Performing_in_Seattle,_Washington.jpg)

Timberlake performing on [the Forget Tomorrow World Tour](/wiki/The_Forget_Tomorrow_World_Tour "The Forget Tomorrow World Tour") in Seattle on May 2, 2024

On January 19, 2024, Timberlake performed a free one-night-only concert at the [Orpheum in Memphis](/wiki/Orpheum_Theatre_(Memphis) "Orpheum Theatre (Memphis)"), where he live-debuted his new single "[Selfish](/wiki/Selfish_(Justin_Timberlake_song) "Selfish (Justin Timberlake song)")" after teasing a new project on social media in previous days.[[219]](#cite_note-219) In promotion of the record, on January 25, 2024, Timberlake appeared on *[The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon](/wiki/The_Tonight_Show_Starring_Jimmy_Fallon "The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon")*; he announced the release of "Selfish" and revealed that the album had taken four years to produce.[[220]](#cite_note-Zemler-220)

"Selfish" debuted at number 19 on the [*Billboard* Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100"), marking Timberlake's highest debut in six years on the chart. It became his 39th career solo entry on the Hot 100 and his 29th to reach the top 40.[[221]](#cite_note-221) On the release day of "Selfish", he also announced that his sixth album, *[Everything I Thought It Was](/wiki/Everything_I_Thought_It_Was "Everything I Thought It Was")*, would be released in March 2024.[[222]](#cite_note-222)[[220]](#cite_note-Zemler-220) Timberlake appeared as a musical guest on the January 27, 2024, episode of *[Saturday Night Live](/wiki/Saturday_Night_Live "Saturday Night Live")*, singing "Selfish" and "Sanctified". On January 30, he appeared on *[The Kelly Clarkson Show](/wiki/The_Kelly_Clarkson_Show "The Kelly Clarkson Show")*[[223]](#cite_note-223)[[224]](#cite_note-224) and held an "intimate" concert at [Irving Plaza](/wiki/Irving_Plaza "Irving Plaza") in [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City") on January 31, his 43rd birthday.[[225]](#cite_note-225) He appeared as the musical guest, where he performed "Selfish" on *[The Graham Norton Show](/wiki/The_Graham_Norton_Show "The Graham Norton Show")*, recorded in [London](/wiki/London "London") on February 22, 2024,[[226]](#cite_note-226) the night before he cancelled *A* *One Night Only* concert, to be held at the [Roundhouse](/wiki/Roundhouse_(venue) "Roundhouse (venue)"), London, on February 23, 2024. Timberlake was recovering from an illness.[[227]](#cite_note-227)[[228]](#cite_note-228) The promotional single "[Drown](/wiki/Drown_(Justin_Timberlake_song) "Drown (Justin Timberlake song)")" was released on February 23, 2024.[[229]](#cite_note-229)[[230]](#cite_note-230)[[231]](#cite_note-231) Timberlake also appeared on [Complex Networks](/wiki/Complex_Networks "Complex Networks") *Sneaker Shopping* on March 11, 2024, and mentioned the release of his new album at the end of the video.[[232]](#cite_note-232)

On March 13, 2024, in anticipation of the album release, Timberlake performed a one-off concert at [The Wiltern](/wiki/The_Wiltern "The Wiltern") in Los Angeles. The show featured a surprise reunion with his NSYNC bandmates, with the group playing a medley of hits and the new album track "Paradise". Timberlake then performed on NPR's Tiny Desk series on March 15 for a half-hour-long concert.[[233]](#cite_note-233) Timberlake also announced the release of a documentary showing the album's creation process.[[234]](#cite_note-234) The second single off the record, "[No Angels](/wiki/No_Angels_(Justin_Timberlake_song) "No Angels (Justin Timberlake song)")", was released on March 15, 2024, the same day as the album's release.[[235]](#cite_note-235) *Everything I Thought It Was* debuted at number four on the U.S. [*Billboard* 200](/wiki/Billboard_200 "Billboard 200"), giving Timberlake his sixth consecutive top five album in the country.[[236]](#cite_note-Ariana_Grande's_'Eternal_Sunshine'-236)

In April 2024, Timberlake opened the [iHeartRadio Music Awards](/wiki/2024_iHeartRadio_Music_Awards "2024 iHeartRadio Music Awards"), performing both singles off the album, "Selfish" and "No Angels".[[237]](#cite_note-237) Timberlake also promoted the album on [the Forget Tomorrow World Tour](/wiki/The_Forget_Tomorrow_World_Tour "The Forget Tomorrow World Tour"), which began on April 29, 2024, in [Vancouver](/wiki/Vancouver "Vancouver"), Canada.[[238]](#cite_note-238) On February 23, 2024, Timberlake announced summer 2024 Europe tour dates.[[239]](#cite_note-239) In March 2024, prior to the tour launching, Trace William Cowen of *[Complex](/wiki/Complex_(magazine) "Complex (magazine)")* reported that the Forget Tomorrow World Tour had become Timberlake's fastest-selling tour to date. They stated that the tour had amassed over $140 million globally in initial ticket sales from over 70 dates and that more than one million attendees are expected. Cowen concluded that "Justin Timberlake's Forget Tomorrow World Tour Is Set to Become His Biggest Yet."[[240]](#cite_note-240) On May 20, 2024, *[Pollstar](/wiki/Pollstar "Pollstar")* announced that nine additional shows were added due to "overwhelming demand" and reported that over 1 million tickets were already sold, becoming Timberlake's fastest selling tour to date.[[241]](#cite_note-241) The tour received generally positive reviews.[[242]](#cite_note-Vancouver_Sun_1-242)

In January 2026, Timberlake performed at the Recording Academy Honours Presented by the Black Music Collective in tribute to Pharrell Williams, who received the Dr. Dre Global Music Icon Award; Timberlake performed his Williams-produced singles "Señorita", "Rock Your Body" and "Like I Love You".[[243]](#cite_note-243)[[244]](#cite_note-244)

## Personal life

### Relationships and children

In early 1999, Timberlake began dating fellow former *[The All New Mickey Mouse Club](/wiki/The_Mickey_Mouse_Club#1989–94_revival:_The_All-New_Mickey_Mouse_Club "The Mickey Mouse Club")* cast member and singer [Britney Spears](/wiki/Britney_Spears "Britney Spears"). Their relationship ended abruptly in March 2002.[[245]](#cite_note-245) Spears wrote in her 2023 memoir, *[The Woman in Me](/wiki/The_Woman_in_Me_(memoir) "The Woman in Me (memoir)")*, that she became pregnant during their relationship and had an abortion in late 2000 after Timberlake said they were not prepared for parenthood and he did not want to be a father.[[246]](#cite_note-246)[[247]](#cite_note-247) In 2003, he briefly dated British singer [Emma Bunton](/wiki/Emma_Bunton "Emma Bunton").[[248]](#cite_note-248)[[249]](#cite_note-249)[[250]](#cite_note-250) In April 2003, he began a relationship with actress [Cameron Diaz](/wiki/Cameron_Diaz "Cameron Diaz") soon after they met at the [Nickelodeon Kids' Choice Awards](/wiki/Nickelodeon_Kids%27_Choice_Awards "Nickelodeon Kids' Choice Awards"). After much speculation of breakups throughout their relationship, the couple split in December 2006 shortly after she introduced him as a musical guest on *[Saturday Night Live](/wiki/Saturday_Night_Live "Saturday Night Live")*.[[251]](#cite_note-251)[[252]](#cite_note-252)[[253]](#cite_note-253)

In January 2007, Timberlake began dating actress [Jessica Biel](/wiki/Jessica_Biel "Jessica Biel").[[254]](#cite_note-254) They became engaged in December 2011 and married on October 19, 2012, at the Borgo Egnazia resort in [Fasano](/wiki/Fasano "Fasano"), Italy.[[255]](#cite_note-255) Their first child, a son, was born in April 2015.[[256]](#cite_note-256)[[257]](#cite_note-257)[[258]](#cite_note-258) In July 2020, they had a second son.[[259]](#cite_note-259)[[260]](#cite_note-260)[[261]](#cite_note-261)

### Legal issues

Shortly after midnight on June 18, 2024, Timberlake was arrested in [Sag Harbor, New York](/wiki/Sag_Harbor,_New_York "Sag Harbor, New York"), for [driving while intoxicated](/wiki/Driving_while_intoxicated "Driving while intoxicated") (DWI).[[262]](#cite_note-arrestandarraignment-262)[[263]](#cite_note-263) In addition to being charged with one count of DWI, Timberlake was issued two citations, one for running a stop sign and one for failure to keep in lane.[[262]](#cite_note-arrestandarraignment-262) He was released nine hours later following his first arraignment at Sag Harbor Village Justice Court.[[262]](#cite_note-arrestandarraignment-262) The Sag Harbor Police Department would later release his mugshot.[[264]](#cite_note-264) Timberlake was scheduled to return to court on July 26, 2024.[[262]](#cite_note-arrestandarraignment-262)[[265]](#cite_note-timberlakearrest-265) According to his arrest report, Timberlake alleged to authorities that he only had one martini shortly before he was pulled over.[[266]](#cite_note-arrestreport-266) Despite making this claim, Timberlake refused a [breathalyzer](/wiki/Breathalyzer "Breathalyzer").[[266]](#cite_note-arrestreport-266)

On June 22, 2024, bartender employees at the hotel where Timberlake was staying before his DWI arrest confirmed to *[People](/wiki/People_(magazine) "People (magazine)")* that he only had one martini while at the hotel bar.[[265]](#cite_note-timberlakearrest-265) One employee stated that "If he was drinking more, it wasn't here."[[265]](#cite_note-timberlakearrest-265) However, according to the arrest report, the officer who pulled Timberlake over after he drove through a stop sign and failed to keep on the right side of the road stated that his "eyes were bloodshot and glassy" at the time and that "A strong odor of an alcoholic beverage was emanating from his breath, he was unable to divide attention, he had slowed speech, he was unsteady afoot, and he performed poorly on all standardized field sobriety tests."[[265]](#cite_note-timberlakearrest-265) During the stop, Timberlake reportedly said, "this is going to ruin the tour." The officer, who did not know who Timberlake was, asked, "what tour?" to which he replied with "the world tour".[[267]](#cite_note-267)[[268]](#cite_note-268)[[269]](#cite_note-269) That exchange went viral, spawning several memes;[[270]](#cite_note-270)[[271]](#cite_note-271)[[272]](#cite_note-272) the reported exchange was not part of the [body camera](/wiki/Body_camera "Body camera") footage of the arrest released in March 2026.[[273]](#cite_note-273)[[274]](#cite_note-274)

At a concert in Chicago on June 21, 2024, Timberlake appeared to address the arrest, saying, "We've been through ups and downs and lefts and rights ... It's been a tough week, but you're here, and I'm here ... I know sometimes I'm hard to love, but you keep on loving me and I love you right back".[[275]](#cite_note-275)[[265]](#cite_note-timberlakearrest-265) Timberlake's lawyer Edward Burke attended his July 26 court arraignment while Timberlake was performing overseas in Poland.[[276]](#cite_note-july26andmore-276) Despite Burke's arguments that Timberlake was not intoxicated at the time of his arrest, Carl Irace, Sag Harbor Village's Justice, ordered for Timberlake to be re-arraigned on August 2.[[277]](#cite_note-august2requirement-277)[[278]](#cite_note-278)[[276]](#cite_note-july26andmore-276) For the August 2 arraignment, Timberlake, who was still on tour, appeared virtually from Antwerp, Belgium, and pled not guilty to a revised misdemeanor charge of driving drunk.[[279]](#cite_note-enterspleaandlicensesuspended-279) Irace also agreed to review Burke's motion to dismiss the DWI charge.[[277]](#cite_note-august2requirement-277) However, Timberlake's New York license was suspended by the judge for an indeterminate amount of time.[[280]](#cite_note-nbcnewslicense-280)

Timberlake was scheduled to have another hearing on August 9,[[279]](#cite_note-enterspleaandlicensesuspended-279) which he was not required to attend.[[281]](#cite_note-281) On September 11, it was revealed that Timberlake would plead guilty to a less serious traffic offense during his September 13 court appearance, with the DWI charge being dropped.[[282]](#cite_note-282)[[283]](#cite_note-283)[[284]](#cite_note-284) Under his plea deal, Timberlake agreed to plead guilty to driving while impaired, a lesser offense that carries a penalty of a $300 to $500 fine and a 90-day driver's license suspension.[[285]](#cite_note-285) On September 13, during an in-person appearance at the Sag Harbor Village Justice Courthouse, Timberlake pled guilty to a noncriminal driving while impaired traffic violation.[[286]](#cite_note-286) Irace then sentenced Timberlake to a $500 fine with a $260 surcharge, 25 hours of community service at the nonprofit of his choosing, and required him to make a public safety announcement.[[287]](#cite_note-pleaandsentencemade-287) It also revealed that Timberlake's refusal to take a breathalyzer automatically triggered a suspension of his driver's license under New York state law.[[287]](#cite_note-pleaandsentencemade-287)

### Residences

In July 2002, Timberlake purchased a 13,250-square-foot (1,231 m2) mansion in [Hollywood Hills](/wiki/Hollywood_Hills "Hollywood Hills") for $8.3 million from [Helen Hunt](/wiki/Helen_Hunt "Helen Hunt").[[288]](#cite_note-288) In 2010, Timberlake purchased a unit at 311 West Broadway in [SoHo](/wiki/SoHo "SoHo"), formerly owned by [Oscar de la Renta](/wiki/Oscar_de_la_Renta "Oscar de la Renta"), for $6.56 million. He sold the unit in 2018 for $6.35 million, incurring a loss.[[289]](#cite_note-289)[[290]](#cite_note-290)

In 2015, Timberlake bought a house in the [Yellowstone Club](/wiki/Yellowstone_Club "Yellowstone Club") near [Big Sky, Montana](/wiki/Big_Sky,_Montana "Big Sky, Montana").[[291]](#cite_note-291)[[292]](#cite_note-292) In May 2017, Timberlake and his wife, [Jessica Biel](/wiki/Jessica_Biel "Jessica Biel"), paid $20.2 million for a 5,375-square-foot (499.4 m2) [penthouse](/wiki/Penthouse_apartment "Penthouse apartment") unit at 443 Greenwich Street in [Tribeca](/wiki/Tribeca "Tribeca"), [Manhattan](/wiki/Manhattan "Manhattan").[[293]](#cite_note-293)

### Earnings
