*[Forbes](/wiki/Forbes "Forbes")* began reporting on Timberlake's earnings in 2008, calculating that he earned $44 million between June 2007 and June 2008 for his music, tour, commercials, and hospitality, making him the world's 4th best-paid music personality at the time, above [Madonna](/wiki/Madonna "Madonna") and [Celine Dion](/wiki/Celine_Dion "Celine Dion").[[294]](#cite_note-294) That year, he was ranked twelfth on the [*Forbes* Celebrity 100](/wiki/Forbes_Celebrity_100 "Forbes Celebrity 100") list[[295]](#cite_note-295) and second on the "Best-Paid Celebrities Under 30" list.[[296]](#cite_note-296)

According to *Billboard*, Timberlake was the third highest-paid musician of 2013, with earnings of $31 million.[[297]](#cite_note-297) He was ranked 26th with earnings of $57 million on the Celebrity 100 list for 2014[[298]](#cite_note-298) and 19th on the 2015 list with $63 million.[[299]](#cite_note-299) Timberlake was listed at number three on the *Billboard* Money-Makers List of 2014.[[300]](#cite_note-300)

He was ranked 41st on the 2019 Celebrity 100 list by *[Forbes](/wiki/Forbes "Forbes")*, with earnings of $57.5 million in 2019.[[301]](#cite_note-301)

### Health

At the end of his [Forget Tomorrow World Tour](/wiki/Forget_Tomorrow_World_Tour "Forget Tomorrow World Tour") in July 2025, Timberlake stated he had been diagnosed with [Lyme disease](/wiki/Lyme_disease "Lyme disease"). He shared in an Instagram post, "When I first got the diagnosis I was shocked for sure. But, at least I could understand why I would be onstage and in a massive amount of nerve pain or, just feeling crazy fatigue or sickness. I was faced with a personal decision. Stop touring? Or, keep going and figure it out. I decided the joy that performing brings me far outweighs the fleeting stress my body was feeling. I'm so glad I kept going".[[302]](#cite_note-302)[[303]](#cite_note-303)

## Artistry

[![Michael Jackson performing](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/46/Michael_Jackson-3.jpg/120px-Michael_Jackson-3.jpg)](/wiki/File:Michael_Jackson-3.jpg)

[![David Bowie performing](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/57/David_Bowie_1997.jpg/250px-David_Bowie_1997.jpg)](/wiki/File:David_Bowie_1997.jpg)

Timberlake has credited musicians such as [Michael Jackson](/wiki/Michael_Jackson "Michael Jackson") (left) and [David Bowie](/wiki/David_Bowie "David Bowie") (right) as his influences.

Timberlake possesses a lyric [tenor](/wiki/Tenor "Tenor") [vocal range](/wiki/Voice_type "Voice type").[[304]](#cite_note-304) He began including [beatboxing](/wiki/Beatboxing "Beatboxing") in his records near the end of his boyband era and into his emergence as a solo act with *Justified* and *FutureSex/LoveSounds*.[[305]](#cite_note-305)[[306]](#cite_note-306)[[307]](#cite_note-307) Many of his songs draw from his personal life and relationships, such as "[Cry Me a River](/wiki/Cry_Me_a_River_(Justin_Timberlake_song) "Cry Me a River (Justin Timberlake song)")" (about his breakup with [Britney Spears](/wiki/Britney_Spears "Britney Spears")).[[308]](#cite_note-308)[[309]](#cite_note-309) Described by critics as a "consummate showman",[[310]](#cite_note-hbjt-310)[[311]](#cite_note-311) Timberlake usually plays guitar, piano, and keyboard in his shows.[[312]](#cite_note-312) Although his music is rooted in [pop](/wiki/Pop_music "Pop music") and [R&B](/wiki/Contemporary_R%26B "Contemporary R&B"),[[13]](#cite_note-allmusicbio-13) *The New York Times*' editor Katie Rogers highlighted Timberlake's crossover appeal after his performance with [Chris Stapleton](/wiki/Chris_Stapleton "Chris Stapleton") at the [2015 Country Music Association Awards](/wiki/49th_Annual_Country_Music_Association_Awards "49th Annual Country Music Association Awards").[[313]](#cite_note-313)

Primarily an R&B album, his 2002 debut, *[Justified](/wiki/Justified_(album) "Justified (album)")*, includes influences from [dance-pop](/wiki/Dance-pop "Dance-pop"), [funk](/wiki/Funk "Funk"), and [soul](/wiki/Soul_music "Soul music") music.[[314]](#cite_note-314) Timberlake has described it as influenced by [Michael Jackson](/wiki/Michael_Jackson "Michael Jackson") and [Stevie Wonder](/wiki/Stevie_Wonder "Stevie Wonder").[[78]](#cite_note-backsexy-78) For the album's supporting tour, the [Justified and Stripped Tour](/wiki/Justified_and_Stripped_Tour "Justified and Stripped Tour"), he co-headlined with [Christina Aguilera](/wiki/Christina_Aguilera "Christina Aguilera"), drawing praise from [Robert Hilburn](/wiki/Robert_Hilburn "Robert Hilburn") of the *[Los Angeles Times](/wiki/Los_Angeles_Times "Los Angeles Times")* for his comfort in performing as part of a package.[[315]](#cite_note-315)

His 2006 follow-up album, *[FutureSex/LoveSounds](/wiki/FutureSex/LoveSounds "FutureSex/LoveSounds")*, departed from his earlier work to explore a broader sound, with elements of [rap](/wiki/Rap_(music) "Rap (music)"), [rock](/wiki/Rock_music "Rock music"), [funk](/wiki/Funk "Funk"), [soul](/wiki/Soul_music "Soul music"), [gospel](/wiki/Gospel_music "Gospel music"), [new wave](/wiki/New_wave_music "New wave music"), [opera](/wiki/Opera "Opera"), and [world music](/wiki/World_music "World music").[[316]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEVineyard2006-316)[[78]](#cite_note-backsexy-78)[[317]](#cite_note-317)[[318]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECollis20071-318) During production, Timberlake was interested in musical techniques associated with rock music, which inspired his approach to recording.[[55]](#cite_note-revs_up-55)[[319]](#cite_note-319) He described the album as drawing from [David Bowie](/wiki/David_Bowie "David Bowie") and [Prince](/wiki/Prince_(musician) "Prince (musician)").[[78]](#cite_note-backsexy-78) Other influences include late [INXS](/wiki/INXS "INXS") frontman [Michael Hutchence](/wiki/Michael_Hutchence "Michael Hutchence"),[[320]](#cite_note-Moss_p_1-320) [Arcade Fire](/wiki/Arcade_Fire "Arcade Fire"), [David Byrne](/wiki/David_Byrne "David Byrne"), [The Killers](/wiki/The_Killers "The Killers"), [The Strokes](/wiki/The_Strokes "The Strokes"), and [Radiohead](/wiki/Radiohead "Radiohead").[[321]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEScaggs20064-321)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/10/Justin_Timberlake_-_The_2020_Experience_World_Tour_-_Charlotte%2C_North_Carolina_04.jpg/330px-Justin_Timberlake_-_The_2020_Experience_World_Tour_-_Charlotte%2C_North_Carolina_04.jpg)](/wiki/File:Justin_Timberlake_-_The_2020_Experience_World_Tour_-_Charlotte,_North_Carolina_04.jpg)

Timberlake performing on his 20/20 Experience Tour in [Charlotte, North Carolina](/wiki/Charlotte,_North_Carolina "Charlotte, North Carolina")

Timberlake's next album, *[The 20/20 Experience](/wiki/The_20/20_Experience "The 20/20 Experience")* (2013), is a neo-soul album partly inspired by the expansive song structures of 1960s and 1970s rock.[[322]](#cite_note-322) Mikael Wood of the *[Los Angeles Times](/wiki/Los_Angeles_Times "Los Angeles Times")* found its elaborate structures ambitious in the vein of Stevie Wonder, Prince, and Michael Jackson.[[323]](#cite_note-Wood-323) He supported the album with a pair of tours: [Legends of the Summer](/wiki/Legends_of_the_Summer_Stadium_Tour "Legends of the Summer Stadium Tour"), which he co-headlined with [Jay-Z](/wiki/Jay-Z "Jay-Z"), and [The 20/20 Experience World Tour](/wiki/The_20/20_Experience_World_Tour "The 20/20 Experience World Tour").[[324]](#cite_note-announcement-324)[[325]](#cite_note-325) These tours introduced his backing ensemble, the Tennessee Kids, a 15-piece multi-instrumentalist throwback to [big band](/wiki/Big_band "Big band") music.[[326]](#cite_note-326)[[327]](#cite_note-327)

## Public image

Timberlake's fashion and style evolution, from "boy-band synchronized wardrobe days" to "a notable source of fashion inspiration to men all over", has been noticed by the media.[[328]](#cite_note-328)[[329]](#cite_note-329) As noted by a *Billboard* editor, "Since his solo career began with the 2002 release of debut album *Justified*, Timberlake has honed his unique sense of style", while citing [Elvis Presley](/wiki/Elvis_Presley "Elvis Presley"), [Johnny Cash](/wiki/Johnny_Cash "Johnny Cash"), [Jerry Lee Lewis](/wiki/Jerry_Lee_Lewis "Jerry Lee Lewis"), and [Frank Sinatra](/wiki/Frank_Sinatra "Frank Sinatra") as style influences: "guys who were just really never trying to be that [stylish], they just were that".[[330]](#cite_note-330) According to American fashion designer [Tom Ford](/wiki/Tom_Ford "Tom Ford"), who has dressed Timberlake since 2011 and created more than 600 exclusive pieces for [The 20/20 Experience World Tour](/wiki/The_20/20_Experience_World_Tour "The 20/20 Experience World Tour"), Timberlake "has a kind of effortless cool that makes classic menswear tailoring modern".[[331]](#cite_note-331)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/2b/Justin_Timberlake_wax_1.jpg/250px-Justin_Timberlake_wax_1.jpg)](/wiki/File:Justin_Timberlake_wax_1.jpg)

Wax statue of Timberlake at [Madame Tussauds](/wiki/Madame_Tussauds "Madame Tussauds") in London

*[The New York Times](/wiki/The_New_York_Times "The New York Times")'* editor Sia Michel wrote in 2007, "Since his last tour, for 2002's multiplatinum *Justified*, he has learned how to project sex-symbol edge" adding, "he's a rock star who can commit."[[332]](#cite_note-Bringing-332)[[333]](#cite_note-333) [Napster](/wiki/Napster "Napster")'s founder, [Sean Parker](/wiki/Sean_Parker "Sean Parker"), portrayed by Timberlake in *[The Social Network](/wiki/The_Social_Network "The Social Network")*, stated, "I don't think I look anything like Timberlake, but it's not so bad being played by a sex symbol."[[334]](#cite_note-334) In [Nielsen Music](/wiki/Nielsen_N.V. "Nielsen N.V.")'s U.S. report for 2015, Timberlake led the "Top 10 Musicians Among [Millennials](/wiki/Millennials "Millennials")" list.[[335]](#cite_note-335) His high-profile relationships earned him the nickname "Trousersnake", a reference to his allegedly big [penis](/wiki/Penis "Penis").[[336]](#cite_note-336)[[337]](#cite_note-337)[[338]](#cite_note-338) Wax statues of Timberlake are on display at the [Madame Tussauds](/wiki/Madame_Tussauds "Madame Tussauds") wax museums in New York,[[339]](#cite_note-339) Las Vegas,[[340]](#cite_note-340) Hollywood,[[341]](#cite_note-341) Nashville,[[342]](#cite_note-342) Berlin,[[343]](#cite_note-343) Amsterdam,[[344]](#cite_note-344) and London.[[345]](#cite_note-345) His costume used in the *[Saturday Night Live](/wiki/Saturday_Night_Live "Saturday Night Live")* skit "[Dick in a Box](/wiki/Dick_in_a_Box "Dick in a Box")" is displayed at "Saturday Night Live: The Exhibition" in New York.[[346]](#cite_note-346)

After New York legalized [same-sex marriage](/wiki/Same-sex_marriage_in_New_York "Same-sex marriage in New York") in 2011, he voiced his support for [LGBT equality](/wiki/LGBT_equality "LGBT equality") in the U.S., stating, "We're people and we're different, all of us. And we should be using our differences to bring ourselves closer together."[[347]](#cite_note-glsen-347)[[348]](#cite_note-348) Timberlake and his wife, [Jessica Biel](/wiki/Jessica_Biel "Jessica Biel"), received the Inspiration Award at the [GLSEN Respect Awards](/wiki/GLSEN_Respect_Awards "GLSEN Respect Awards") in 2015, with the executive director saying, "They are two vocal and committed [allies](/wiki/Straight_ally "Straight ally") to the LGBT community who are also devoted to charitable works that improve the lives of youth."[[347]](#cite_note-glsen-347)

Timberlake's pronunciation of the word "me" on "[It's Gonna Be Me](/wiki/It%27s_Gonna_Be_Me "It's Gonna Be Me")" was popularized as an [internet meme](/wiki/Internet_meme "Internet meme") titled "It's Gonna Be May", after a [Tumblr](/wiki/Tumblr "Tumblr") image of Timberlake was posted in 2012 with the respective caption.[[349]](#cite_note-349) The meme eventually became an annually recurring joke throughout the month of April,[[350]](#cite_note-Vox-350)[[351]](#cite_note-351) which gained the attention of [Barack Obama](/wiki/Barack_Obama "Barack Obama") and Timberlake himself.[[352]](#cite_note-352) Influenced by the national attention received by Timberlake's selfie inside a [voting booth](/wiki/Voting_booth "Voting booth") shared on [Instagram](/wiki/Instagram "Instagram") during the 2016 presidential election, which was at the moment an illegal act,[[353]](#cite_note-353) Senator [Brian Kelsey](/wiki/Brian_Kelsey "Brian Kelsey") of [Germantown, Tennessee](/wiki/Germantown,_Tennessee "Germantown, Tennessee") brought up a bill that would allow taking photos in voting poll stations, with some exceptions against bad procedures. It was later approved by the [Tennessee Senate](/wiki/Tennessee_Senate "Tennessee Senate").[[354]](#cite_note-354)

In June 2021, Timberlake spoke out to support Britney Spears during her [court battle over her conservatorship](/wiki/Britney_Spears_conservatorship_dispute "Britney Spears conservatorship dispute"). Timberlake wrote on his Twitter that "No woman should ever be restricted from making decisions about her own body [...] [Jessica Biel] and I send our love, and our absolute support to Britney during this time. We hope the courts, and her family make this right and let her live however she wants to live".[[355]](#cite_note-355)

### Controversies

Following the release of *[Framing Britney Spears](/wiki/Framing_Britney_Spears "Framing Britney Spears")*, public attention was brought to old comments Timberlake made in 2002 to describe his relationship with [Britney Spears](/wiki/Britney_Spears "Britney Spears") following their breakup, with some considering them to contain misogynistic rhetoric.[[356]](#cite_note-356) It also led to renewed interest in his participation in the [Super Bowl XXXVIII halftime show controversy](/wiki/Super_Bowl_XXXVIII_halftime_show_controversy "Super Bowl XXXVIII halftime show controversy"), where he exposed [Janet Jackson](/wiki/Janet_Jackson "Janet Jackson")'s breast on live television. Following much public pressure, Timberlake issued a public apology on his Instagram page, writing he "benefited from a system that condones misogyny and racism" and "I do not want to ever benefit from others being pulled down again." He finished by saying, "I care deeply about the wellbeing of the people I love and have loved. I can do better and I will do better".[[357]](#cite_note-357)

Public scrutiny surrounding the Super Bowl XXXVIII incident was raised when a stylist alleged that the plan was originally for Timberlake to step on the back of Jackson's dress "to reveal her butt in this pearl g-string" but that he changed the plan to exposing her breast because he wanted a "reveal" to "one-up" Britney Spears' and [Madonna](/wiki/Madonna "Madonna")'s kiss at the [2003 MTV Video Music Awards](/wiki/2003_MTV_Video_Music_Awards "2003 MTV Video Music Awards").[[358]](#cite_note-358)[[359]](#cite_note-359)[[360]](#cite_note-360) The claim that Timberlake wanted the breast reveal, however, is contradictory to a statement from Salli Frattini, the producer of the halftime show, who stated in 2018 that the production team experimented with removing elements of clothing in rehearsal to reveal Jackson's skirt without nudity but that the idea was ultimately nixed. According to Frattini, it was Jackson's team who pitched the idea to Timberlake prior to the show that led to Jackson's breast being exposed.[[361]](#cite_note-361)

## Legacy

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/63/Justin_Timberlake_and_Steve_Cropper_performing_at_the_White_House.jpg/500px-Justin_Timberlake_and_Steve_Cropper_performing_at_the_White_House.jpg)](/wiki/File:Justin_Timberlake_and_Steve_Cropper_performing_at_the_White_House.jpg)

Timberlake *(center)* and [Steve Cropper](/wiki/Steve_Cropper "Steve Cropper") performing at the White House, 2013, with President [Barack Obama](/wiki/Barack_Obama "Barack Obama") on the left.

Timberlake has been referred to as the "[President of Pop](/wiki/Honorific_nicknames_in_popular_music "Honorific nicknames in popular music")"[[362]](#cite_note-362) and "Prince of Pop"[[363]](#cite_note-363) by contemporary journalists.[[364]](#cite_note-364) In a 2016 article in *[The Hollywood Reporter](/wiki/The_Hollywood_Reporter "The Hollywood Reporter")*, editor Scott Feinberg stated Timberlake is "widely regarded as one of the greatest all-around entertainers in the history of show business".[[365]](#cite_note-365)

In 2003, *[Rolling Stone](/wiki/Rolling_Stone "Rolling Stone")* named him the biggest pop star of the year and featured him on the magazine's cover, declaring he was "The New King of Pop".[[11]](#cite_note-rsinterview-11) For *[Entertainment Weekly](/wiki/Entertainment_Weekly "Entertainment Weekly")*, his second album, *FutureSex/LoveSounds*, "redefined pop's cutting edge";[[366]](#cite_note-ewdecade-366) for *[Vibe](/wiki/Vibe_(magazine) "Vibe (magazine)")*, it "pushed boundaries more forcefully" than works by his male contemporaries.[[367]](#cite_note-vibe-367) [Fuse TV](/wiki/Fuse_TV "Fuse TV")'s editor Jason Lipshutz stated it "changed the game. [The album] was steely and sweaty, a universal dance opus that made room for intimacy. It had the best first half of any pop album in 25 years. You have to go back to 1979 for *[Off the Wall](/wiki/Off_the_Wall "Off the Wall")* to find a pop album with a first half that matches up."[[368]](#cite_note-368) He was ranked 66th on the [VH1](/wiki/VH1 "VH1") 100 Greatest Artists of All Time.[[369]](#cite_note-369) Timberlake appeared for the first time on the [Time 100](/wiki/Time_100 "Time 100") list of most influential people by *[Time](/wiki/Time_(magazine) "Time (magazine)")* in 2007.[[370]](#cite_note-370) In 2013, he made his second appearance on the 100 list, with [Stevie Wonder](/wiki/Stevie_Wonder "Stevie Wonder") writing, "Justin has accomplished a lot at a young age, taking advantage of all the possibilities, and yet he's found time to give of himself too—he gets and gives back. He has a spirit. He does God's work through using the most of his talent."[[371]](#cite_note-time2013-371)

*Justified'*s single "[Cry Me a River](/wiki/Cry_Me_a_River_(Justin_Timberlake_song) "Cry Me a River (Justin Timberlake song)")" was ranked at number 20 on *[Rolling Stone](/wiki/Rolling_Stone "Rolling Stone")'s* 100 Best Songs of the 2000s and one of their [500 greatest songs of all time](/wiki/Rolling_Stone%27s_500_Greatest_Songs_of_All_Time "Rolling Stone's 500 Greatest Songs of All Time").[[372]](#cite_note-372)[[373]](#cite_note-373) *[Billboard](/wiki/Billboard_(magazine) "Billboard (magazine)")* editor Jason Lipshutz named the song's music video "one of the more brilliant musical moments in pop music since the dawn of the century".[[374]](#cite_note-374) His second album, *[FutureSex/LoveSounds](/wiki/FutureSex/LoveSounds "FutureSex/LoveSounds")*, was placed at 46 on *Rolling Stone*'s 100 Best Albums of the 2000s,[[375]](#cite_note-375) the ninth best album of the decade for *[Entertainment Weekly](/wiki/Entertainment_Weekly "Entertainment Weekly")*,[[366]](#cite_note-ewdecade-366) and the greatest of the decade for *[Vibe](/wiki/Vibe_(magazine) "Vibe (magazine)")*.[[367]](#cite_note-vibe-367) The lead single, "[SexyBack](/wiki/SexyBack "SexyBack")", helped introduce [EDM](/wiki/Electronic_Dance_Music "Electronic Dance Music") sounds to [top 40 radio](/wiki/Top_40_radio "Top 40 radio"), as it brought together variations of electronic dance music with Timberlake's R&B tone.[[376]](#cite_note-376)[[377]](#cite_note-377) Aside from earning critical acclaim for its parent album, Sia Michel of *[The New York Times](/wiki/The_New_York_Times "The New York Times")* noted that he was responsible for popularizing in 2006 the catchphrase "I'm bringing sexy back".[[318]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECollis20071-318)[[332]](#cite_note-Bringing-332)[[378]](#cite_note-378)

Considered a [pop icon](/wiki/Pop_icon "Pop icon") by media outlets,[[379]](#cite_note-379) his work has influenced numerous artists. This includes [Justin Bieber](/wiki/Justin_Bieber "Justin Bieber"),[[380]](#cite_note-380) [Shawn Mendes](/wiki/Shawn_Mendes "Shawn Mendes"),[[381]](#cite_note-381) [Olly Murs](/wiki/Olly_Murs "Olly Murs"),[[382]](#cite_note-382) [Maroon 5](/wiki/Maroon_5 "Maroon 5"),[[383]](#cite_note-383) [Britney Spears](/wiki/Britney_Spears "Britney Spears"),[[384]](#cite_note-384) [Lorde](/wiki/Lorde "Lorde"),[[385]](#cite_note-385) [Marilyn Manson](/wiki/Marilyn_Manson "Marilyn Manson"),[[386]](#cite_note-386) [Joe Jonas](/wiki/Joe_Jonas "Joe Jonas"),[[387]](#cite_note-387) [Thomas Rhett](/wiki/Thomas_Rhett "Thomas Rhett"),[[388]](#cite_note-388) [Ed Sheeran](/wiki/Ed_Sheeran "Ed Sheeran"),[[389]](#cite_note-389) [Jason Derulo](/wiki/Jason_Derulo "Jason Derulo"),[[390]](#cite_note-390) [Tori Kelly](/wiki/Tori_Kelly "Tori Kelly"),[[391]](#cite_note-391) [Liam Payne](/wiki/Liam_Payne "Liam Payne"),[[392]](#cite_note-392)[[393]](#cite_note-393) [Bridgit Mendler](/wiki/Bridgit_Mendler "Bridgit Mendler"),[[394]](#cite_note-394) [Hunter Hayes](/wiki/Hunter_Hayes "Hunter Hayes"),[[395]](#cite_note-395) [BoA](/wiki/BoA "BoA"),[[396]](#cite_note-396) [Seungri](/wiki/Seungri "Seungri"),[[397]](#cite_note-397)[[398]](#cite_note-398) [Taeyang](/wiki/Taeyang "Taeyang"),[[399]](#cite_note-399) [Rain](/wiki/Rain_(entertainer) "Rain (entertainer)"),[[400]](#cite_note-400)[[401]](#cite_note-401) [Lloyd Banks](/wiki/Lloyd_Banks "Lloyd Banks"),[[402]](#cite_note-402) [Maluma](/wiki/Maluma "Maluma"),[[403]](#cite_note-403) [Rosalía](/wiki/Rosal%C3%ADa "Rosalía"),[[404]](#cite_note-404) and [Jungkook](/wiki/Jungkook "Jungkook").[[405]](#cite_note-405) Bieber and [Nick Jonas](/wiki/Nick_Jonas "Nick Jonas") have cited him as one of their role models, with the latter stating it is for "not only transitioning from where he started, but also balancing acting and singing".[[406]](#cite_note-406)[[407]](#cite_note-407) Christian hip hop artist [TobyMac](/wiki/TobyMac "TobyMac") has stated Timberlake's work inspires him, commenting, "he's setting himself up to be a classic, making decisions and moving on them. That's a great place to be."[[408]](#cite_note-408) In the context of male artists that achieved commercial success after leaving their boy bands, Brittany Spanos from *[Rolling Stone](/wiki/Rolling_Stone "Rolling Stone")* wrote, "Timberlake and [Michael Jackson](/wiki/Michael_Jackson "Michael Jackson") set a high bar for what could be attained by solo success in that they not only scored numerous number-one hits but they also crafted the mold for what it meant to be a male pop star",[[409]](#cite_note-409) while for *[Variety](/wiki/Variety_(magazine) "Variety (magazine)")*'s Jeremy Blacklow, the singer is "the modern case study".[[410]](#cite_note-410) Multiple music publications have deemed *Justified* as the standard for post-boy-band solo albums and teen pop stars seeking credibility.[[411]](#cite_note-411)[[412]](#cite_note-412)[[413]](#cite_note-413) *[Billboard](/wiki/Billboard_(magazine) "Billboard (magazine)")* critics discussed in 2018 whether Timberlake is "the Best Male Pop Star of the 21st century;" those in favor named his crossover appeal, career longevity, showmanship, and credibility within the industry among the reasons.[[414]](#cite_note-414)

## Achievements

See also: [List of awards and nominations received by Justin Timberlake](/wiki/List_of_awards_and_nominations_received_by_Justin_Timberlake "List of awards and nominations received by Justin Timberlake")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/fa/Timberlake_Pilgrimage_Festival.jpg/250px-Timberlake_Pilgrimage_Festival.jpg)](/wiki/File:Timberlake_Pilgrimage_Festival.jpg)

Timberlake at the 2017 [Pilgrimage Music & Cultural Festival](/wiki/Pilgrimage_Music_%26_Cultural_Festival "Pilgrimage Music & Cultural Festival")

Throughout his solo career, Timberlake has sold over 32 million albums and 56 million singles globally and a further 70 million records with NSYNC, making him one of the [world's best-selling music artists](/wiki/World%27s_best-selling_music_artists "World's best-selling music artists").[[33]](#cite_note-nsync_records-33)[[415]](#cite_note-415) Timberlake has won ten [Grammy Awards](/wiki/Grammy_Award "Grammy Award"), four [Emmy Awards](/wiki/Emmy_Award "Emmy Award"), seven [American Music Awards](/wiki/American_Music_Award "American Music Award"), three [Brit Awards](/wiki/Brit_Awards "Brit Awards"), nine [*Billboard* Music Awards](/wiki/Billboard_Music_Awards "Billboard Music Awards"), and eleven [MTV Video Music Awards](/wiki/MTV_Video_Music_Award "MTV Video Music Award"). His Grammy wins include [categories](/wiki/List_of_Grammy_Award_categories "List of Grammy Award categories") in the pop, dance, and R&B genres,[[416]](#cite_note-416) while his Emmy wins consist of two [Outstanding Original Music and Lyrics](/wiki/Primetime_Emmy_Award_for_Outstanding_Original_Music_and_Lyrics "Primetime Emmy Award for Outstanding Original Music and Lyrics") and two [Outstanding Guest Actor in a Comedy Series](/wiki/Primetime_Emmy_Award_for_Outstanding_Guest_Actor_in_a_Comedy_Series "Primetime Emmy Award for Outstanding Guest Actor in a Comedy Series").[[417]](#cite_note-417) Timberlake received the [Michael Jackson Video Vanguard Award](/wiki/Michael_Jackson_Video_Vanguard_Award "Michael Jackson Video Vanguard Award") at the [2013 MTV Video Music Awards](/wiki/2013_MTV_Video_Music_Awards "2013 MTV Video Music Awards")[[138]](#cite_note-vanguard-138) and the [Innovator Award](/wiki/IHeartRadio_Music_Awards#iHeartRadio_Innovator_Award "IHeartRadio Music Awards") at the [2015 iHeartRadio Music Awards](/wiki/2015_iHeartRadio_Music_Awards "2015 iHeartRadio Music Awards").[[418]](#cite_note-newsiheart-418) Among other awards, he won the [MTV Video Music Award for Video of the Year](/wiki/MTV_Video_Music_Award_for_Video_of_the_Year "MTV Video Music Award for Video of the Year") for "Mirrors" in 2013 and the [*Billboard* Music Award](/wiki/Billboard_Music_Award "Billboard Music Award") for [Top Artist](/wiki/Billboard_Music_Award_for_Top_Artist "Billboard Music Award for Top Artist") with the [Top Billboard 200 Album](/wiki/Billboard_Music_Award_for_Top_Billboard_200_Album "Billboard Music Award for Top Billboard 200 Album") for *The 20/20 Experience* in 2014. Timberlake received the inaugural Decade Award at the [2016 Teen Choice Awards](/wiki/2016_Teen_Choice_Awards "2016 Teen Choice Awards") for his continuous achievements since the release of *[FutureSex/LoveSounds](/wiki/FutureSex/LoveSounds "FutureSex/LoveSounds")* (2006).[[419]](#cite_note-419)

In October 2015, he was inducted into the [Memphis Music Hall of Fame](/wiki/Memphis_Music_Hall_of_Fame "Memphis Music Hall of Fame"), becoming its youngest member.[[420]](#cite_note-420)[[421]](#cite_note-421) On April 30, 2018, Timberlake reunited with his NSYNC bandmates to receive a star on the [Hollywood Walk of Fame](/wiki/Hollywood_Walk_of_Fame "Hollywood Walk of Fame").[[422]](#cite_note-422) In 2019, Timberlake received a Contemporary Icon Award from the [Songwriters Hall of Fame](/wiki/Songwriters_Hall_of_Fame "Songwriters Hall of Fame")[[423]](#cite_note-423) and an honorary [Doctor of Music](/wiki/Doctor_of_Music "Doctor of Music") degree from [Berklee College of Music](/wiki/Berklee_College_of_Music "Berklee College of Music").[[424]](#cite_note-424)

All five of Timberlake's albums have been certified [platinum](/wiki/Music_recording_sales_certification "Music recording sales certification") or better by the [RIAA](/wiki/RIAA "RIAA") and have received numerous awards. Worldwide sales figures for *[Justified](/wiki/Justified_(album) "Justified (album)")* stand at 10 million copies,[[425]](#cite_note-425) *[FutureSex/LoveSounds](/wiki/FutureSex/LoveSounds "FutureSex/LoveSounds")* at 10 million,[[426]](#cite_note-426) and joint sales of *[The 20/20 Experience](/wiki/The_20/20_Experience "The 20/20 Experience")* and *[2 of 2](/wiki/The_20/20_Experience_%E2%80%93_2_of_2 "The 20/20 Experience – 2 of 2")* at 6 million copies.[[427]](#cite_note-427) As of 2014[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Justin_Timberlake&action=edit), Timberlake has had seven songs exceed 3 million digital downloads in the United States, with "[SexyBack](/wiki/SexyBack "SexyBack")" (4.5),[[428]](#cite_note-428) "[4 Minutes](/wiki/4_Minutes "4 Minutes")",[[429]](#cite_note-threem-429) "[Dead and Gone](/wiki/Dead_and_Gone "Dead and Gone")",[[429]](#cite_note-threem-429) "[Suit & Tie](/wiki/Suit_%26_Tie "Suit & Tie")",[[430]](#cite_note-Grein-430) "[Mirrors](/wiki/Mirrors_(Justin_Timberlake_song) "Mirrors (Justin Timberlake song)")",[[431]](#cite_note-431) "[Holy Grail](/wiki/Holy_Grail_(Jay_Z_song) "Holy Grail (Jay Z song)")",[[430]](#cite_note-Grein-430) and "[Can't Stop the Feeling!](/wiki/Can%27t_Stop_the_Feeling! "Can't Stop the Feeling!")".[[432]](#cite_note-432)

According to *Billboard*, [FutureSex/LoveShow](/wiki/FutureSex/LoveShow "FutureSex/LoveShow") was the third highest-grossing concert tour of 2007 and the highest solo.[[433]](#cite_note-433) [The 20/20 Experience World Tour](/wiki/The_20/20_Experience_World_Tour "The 20/20 Experience World Tour") was an international success and became Timberlake's most successful tour to date. The tour was the highest-grossing led by a solo artist in 2014 and one of the [highest-grossing tours of the decade](/wiki/Highest_grossing_tours "Highest grossing tours").[[434]](#cite_note-434) For its associated album, *[The 20/20 Experience](/wiki/The_20/20_Experience "The 20/20 Experience")*, Timberlake was named 2013 Artist of the Year with the top-selling album by [iTunes](/wiki/ITunes "ITunes")' annual list of bestsellers.[[435]](#cite_note-435)

In the United States, five of Timberlake's singles have topped the [*Billboard* Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100"): "[SexyBack](/wiki/SexyBack "SexyBack")", "[My Love](/wiki/My_Love_(Justin_Timberlake_song) "My Love (Justin Timberlake song)")" and "[What Goes Around... Comes Around](/wiki/What_Goes_Around..._Comes_Around "What Goes Around... Comes Around")", "[Give It to Me](/wiki/Give_It_to_Me_(Timbaland_song) "Give It to Me (Timbaland song)")" and "Can't Stop the Feeling!".[[165]](#cite_note-hot100numberone-165) The latter became the 41st Hot 100 number one song to be nominated for an [Academy Award for Best Original Song](/wiki/Academy_Award_for_Best_Original_Song "Academy Award for Best Original Song").[[436]](#cite_note-436) He topped nine [*Billboard* Year-End charts](/wiki/Billboard_Year-End "Billboard Year-End") for 2013, including *Billboard* 200 Artists[[437]](#cite_note-437) and *Billboard* 200 Albums.[[438]](#cite_note-438) For 2014, Timberlake was named *Billboard* Top Male Artist.[[439]](#cite_note-439) *Billboard* published a list of "Greatest of All-Time [Pop Songs](/wiki/Pop_Songs "Pop Songs") Artists" in 2017, where Timberlake ranked at number 5, being the top male soloist.[[440]](#cite_note-440) The magazine also ranked him 25th on their "The Top 60 Male Artists of All-Time" list in 2018[[441]](#cite_note-441) and 64th on "The Hot 100's Top Artists of All Time".[[442]](#cite_note-442) In 2019, *Billboard* ranked him 20th on their decade-end chart for "Top Artists" of the 2010s[[443]](#cite_note-443) and 74th on the "Top 125 Greatest of All-Time Artists Chart".[[444]](#cite_note-444)

## Other ventures

### Business ventures

Timberlake has co-owned or provided celebrity endorsement for three restaurants in the United States: "Chi" opened in [West Hollywood, California](/wiki/West_Hollywood,_California "West Hollywood, California"), in 2003,[[445]](#cite_note-445) and "Destino" and "Southern Hospitality" in New York opened in 2006 and 2007, respectively.[[446]](#cite_note-446) In 2005, Timberlake launched the [William Rast](/wiki/William_Rast "William Rast") clothing line with childhood friend Juan ("Trace") Ayala. The 2007 line contained cord jackets, cashmere sweaters, jeans, and polo shirts.[[447]](#cite_note-447) The pair reports inspiration from fellow Memphis resident [Elvis Presley](/wiki/Elvis_Presley "Elvis Presley"): "Elvis is the perfect mixture of Justin and I", Ayala says. "You can go back and see pictures of him in cowboy boots and a cowboy hat and a nice button-down shirt, but then again you can see him in a tux and a collared shirt with rhinestones on it and slacks. We like to think 'If he was alive today, what would he be wearing?'"[[448]](#cite_note-448) Target has announced that a William Rast collection, including denim, outerwear and sportswear for men and women, would launch in December and be available for a month.[[449]](#cite_note-449) In 2015, the clothing line earned him a [Lord & Taylor](/wiki/Lord_%26_Taylor "Lord & Taylor")'s Fashion Oracle Award at the [Fashion Group International](/wiki/Fashion_Group_International "Fashion Group International")'s *Night of The Stars Gala*.[[450]](#cite_note-450) An avid amateur golfer, in 2007 Timberlake purchased the run-down Big Creek Golf Course in his hometown of [Millington, Tennessee](/wiki/Millington,_Tennessee "Millington, Tennessee"), which he redeveloped as the [eco-friendly](/wiki/Eco-friendly "Eco-friendly") Mirimichi Golf Course at the cost of around $US16 million. It was reopened on July 25, 2009, but closed again on January 15, 2010, for further improvements expected to take six months.[[451]](#cite_note-451) In October 2011, Timberlake received the Futures Award at the [Environmental Media Awards](/wiki/Environmental_Media_Awards "Environmental Media Awards") for his green-conscious golf course.[[452]](#cite_note-452) It was reported on November 7, 2014, that Timberlake had sold Mirimichi to Three Star Leasing LLC for $500,000.[[453]](#cite_note-453) In October 2018, Timberlake and [Levi's](/wiki/Levi%27s "Levi's") debuted their collaborative clothing line collection "Fresh Leaves".[[454]](#cite_note-454) In 2022, Justin Timberlake invested in Greenville, South Carolina par-3 golf course Greenville 3's.[[455]](#cite_note-455)

Timberlake provides celebrity endorsement for many commercial products, this aspect of his business being managed by [IMG](/wiki/IMG_(company) "IMG (company)") since April 2008.[[456]](#cite_note-456) Major endorsements in 2009 included [Sony](/wiki/Sony "Sony") electronic products,[[457]](#cite_note-457) [Givenchy](/wiki/Givenchy "Givenchy")'s men's fragrance "[Play](/wiki/Parfums_Givenchy#Fragrances_for_men "Parfums Givenchy")",[[458]](#cite_note-458) the [Audi A1](/wiki/Audi_A1 "Audi A1"),[[459]](#cite_note-459) and [Callaway Golf Company](/wiki/Callaway_Golf_Company "Callaway Golf Company") products,[[460]](#cite_note-460)[[461]](#cite_note-461) and in 2011, [Myspace](/wiki/Myspace "Myspace").[[462]](#cite_note-462)[[463]](#cite_note-463) In 2011, he teamed up with [Capital One](/wiki/Capital_One "Capital One") to announce the winners of the inaugural Capital One Cup in a six-minute advertisement, where he is repeatedly getting hit in the groin.[[464]](#cite_note-464) In 2012, he hosted [Walmart](/wiki/Walmart "Walmart")'s annual shareholders meeting,[[465]](#cite_note-465) saying, "I buy a lot at Walmart."[[466]](#cite_note-466)

In 2014, Timberlake partnered with [Sauza Tequila](/wiki/Sauza_Tequila "Sauza Tequila") to relaunch his own version of the beverage as part of the Sauza franchise: Sauza 901.[[467]](#cite_note-467) In 2016, he became an investor in the beverage company [Bai Brands](/wiki/Bai_Brands "Bai Brands").[[468]](#cite_note-468) In 2017, [Tiger Woods](/wiki/Tiger_Woods "Tiger Woods") and Timberlake acquired an ownership stake in the Hurricane Junior Golf Tour.[[469]](#cite_note-469)

Timberlake and his wife, Jessica Biel, are minority owners of the [Memphis Grizzlies](/wiki/Memphis_Grizzlies "Memphis Grizzlies").[[470]](#cite_note-470)[[471]](#cite_note-471)

Timberlake is an [Air Jordan](/wiki/Air_Jordan "Air Jordan") brand ambassador. His first collection with the brand, the Legend of the Summer collection, debuted on the co-headlining [tour](/wiki/Legends_of_the_Summer_Stadium_Tour "Legends of the Summer Stadium Tour") of the same name with Jay-Z. These sneakers have gone on to resell for upwards of $10,000.[[472]](#cite_note-472) In the lead-up to his 2018 Super Bowl Halftime Show, Timberlake worked with famed [Nike, Inc.](/wiki/Nike,_Inc. "Nike, Inc.") designer [Tinker Hatfield](/wiki/Tinker_Hatfield "Tinker Hatfield") to design his own version of the Air Jordan 3.[[473]](#cite_note-473) Timberlake debuted the shoe at the halftime show. Timberlake's performance was estimated to be worth $2.86 million in marketing for Nike and is credited for reigniting interest in the Nike and Air Jordan brands.[[474]](#cite_note-474)[[475]](#cite_note-475) Nike went on to release several colorways as part of the collaboration with Timberlake.[[476]](#cite_note-476)[[477]](#cite_note-477)

In May 2022, Timberlake sold the rights to his entire musical catalog, around 200 songs he wrote or co-wrote, to [Hipgnosis](/wiki/Hipgnosis_Songs_Fund "Hipgnosis Songs Fund") Song Management for $100 million. The deal covers only pre-existing work and not work he produces after the deal.[[478]](#cite_note-478)[[479]](#cite_note-479)

Timberlake had an estimated net worth of $250 million prior to the Hipgnosis deal and is now estimated to be worth in excess of $350 million—making him one of the music industry's wealthiest solo performers.[[480]](#cite_note-480)

### Philanthropy

Timberlake has been active in several charitable pursuits, initially through NSYNC's "Challenge for the Children" aimed at a range of charities, and since 2001 through his "Justin Timberlake Foundation", which initially funded music education programs in schools but now has a much broader agenda.[[481]](#cite_note-481) In October 2005, the Grammy Association presented Timberlake with an award for his humanitarian efforts in Tennessee, alongside writer/director [Craig Brewer](/wiki/Craig_Brewer "Craig Brewer"), also a Memphis native.[[482]](#cite_note-482)

In November 2007, he donated $100,000 from takings from his Australian tour to [Wildlife Warriors](/wiki/Wildlife_Warriors "Wildlife Warriors"), founded by [Steve Irwin](/wiki/Steve_Irwin "Steve Irwin").[[483]](#cite_note-483) On March 23, 2008, he donated $100,000 to the [Memphis Rock N' Soul Museum](/wiki/Memphis_Rock_N%27_Soul_Museum "Memphis Rock N' Soul Museum") and another $100,000 to the Memphis Music Foundation.[[484]](#cite_note-484)

On November 12, 2007, the [PGA Tour](/wiki/PGA_Tour "PGA Tour") announced that Timberlake, an avid golfer who plays to a six [handicap](/wiki/Handicap_(golf) "Handicap (golf)"),[[485]](#cite_note-485) would become the host of the tour's [Las Vegas](/wiki/Las_Vegas "Las Vegas") tournament starting in 2008. With Timberlake's agreement to host the tournament, its name was changed to the [Justin Timberlake Shriners Hospitals for Children Open](/wiki/Justin_Timberlake_Shriners_Hospitals_for_Children_Open "Justin Timberlake Shriners Hospitals for Children Open"). He played in the celebrity pro-am on the day before the competitive tournament and hosted a charity concert during the week of the tournament.[[486]](#cite_note-486) The activity was a success and was repeated in 2009. A review of the value of celebrities to fundraising concluded that Timberlake's contribution to Shriners Hospitals for Children was the single most valuable celebrity endorsement in the U.S. during 2009 and worth over $US9 million.[[487]](#cite_note-487) However, in 2012, the event's chair, Raoul Frevel, told reporters Timberlake would no longer be involved in the event: "We tried everything we could to get him more involved with our kids and the hospitals. But it seemed that when the TV cameras weren't on, he disappeared."[[488]](#cite_note-488)

[![A young man dressed in black shirt and pants is walking while holding a golf stick](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/18/JustinTimberlake.jpg/250px-JustinTimberlake.jpg)](/wiki/File:JustinTimberlake.jpg)

Timberlake in 2006

Timberlake often participates in the [American Century Championship](/wiki/American_Century_Championship "American Century Championship")[[489]](#cite_note-489) and the [AT&T Pebble Beach Pro-Am](/wiki/AT%26T_Pebble_Beach_Pro-Am "AT&T Pebble Beach Pro-Am").[[490]](#cite_note-490) Both annual tournaments raise money for a range of national and local nonprofits.[[491]](#cite_note-491)[[492]](#cite_note-492)

In late 2012, Timberlake and his wife Jessica Biel volunteered in Far Rockaway, Queens over the weekend after [Hurricane Sandy](/wiki/Hurricane_Sandy "Hurricane Sandy"). Joined by their friend Timbaland, the couple helped distribute backpacks of supplies to members of the community who were still struggling after the hurricane.[[493]](#cite_note-493)

Timberlake has donated items for auction for several charities, including by [Ten O'Clock Classics](/wiki/Ten_O%27Clock_Classics "Ten O'Clock Classics")[[494]](#cite_note-494) and [MusiCares](/wiki/MusiCares "MusiCares").[[495]](#cite_note-495) In 2010, Timberlake participated in the [Hope for Haiti Now](/wiki/Hope_for_Haiti_Now "Hope for Haiti Now") telethon performing "[Hallelujah](/wiki/Hallelujah_(Leonard_Cohen_song) "Hallelujah (Leonard Cohen song)")". The cover was made available for digital download with all proceeds donated to organizations doing relief work in Haiti.[[496]](#cite_note-496) In 2016, Timberlake was featured in a new version of "[Where Is the Love?](/wiki/Where_Is_the_Love%3F "Where Is the Love?")". The proceeds of the charity single will go to educational programs.[[497]](#cite_note-497) In 2017, Timberlake and [Eminem](/wiki/Eminem "Eminem") helped raise over $2 million for [Manchester Arena bombing](/wiki/Manchester_Arena_bombing "Manchester Arena bombing") victims.[[498]](#cite_note-498)

### Book

Timberlake's autobiography, *[Hindsight & All the Things I Can't See In Front of Me](/wiki/Hindsight_(book) "Hindsight (book)")*,[[499]](#cite_note-499) was released on October 30, 2018.[[500]](#cite_note-ew1-500) The book covers some on- and off-camera candid images, the singer's early life, and inspiration for songs. According to publisher HarperCollins, *Hindsight* includes a "collection of anecdotes, reflections, and observations on his life and work".[[500]](#cite_note-ew1-500)
